name: Delete ACL Rule
description: Deletes an access control rule from a Google Calendar. Use when you need to remove sharing permissions for a user, group, or domain.

inputs.name: rule_id
inputs.description: ACL rule identifier.

inputs.name: calendar_id
inputs.description: Calendar identifier. To retrieve calendar IDs call the calendarList.list method. If you want to access the primary calendar of the currently logged in user, use the "primary" keyword.

requiredInputs.name: rule_id
requiredInputs.description: ACL rule identifier.

requiredInputs.name: calendar_id
requiredInputs.description: Calendar identifier. To retrieve calendar IDs call the calendarList.list method. If you want to access the primary calendar of the currently logged in user, use the "primary" keyword.

outputs.name: data
outputs.description: Data from the action execution

outputs.name: error
outputs.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

outputs.name: successful
outputs.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

name: Get ACL Rule
description: Retrieves a specific access control rule for a calendar. Use when you need to check permissions for a specific user, group, or domain.

inputs.name: rule_id
inputs.description: ACL rule identifier. Format: 'scope_type:scope_value' or 'default'. Valid scope types: 'user' (email), 'group' (group email), 'domain' (domain name), 'default' (public access). Examples: 'user:john@example.com', 'group:team@example.com', 'domain:example.com', 'default'. Note: 'me' is NOT valid; use actual email/domain. The rule must exist - use GOOGLECALENDAR_LIST_ACL_RULES to find valid IDs.

inputs.name: calendar_id
inputs.description: Calendar identifier. To retrieve calendar IDs call the calendarList.list method. If you want to access the primary calendar of the currently logged in user, use the "primary" keyword.

requiredInputs.name: rule_id
requiredInputs.description: ACL rule identifier. Format: 'scope_type:scope_value' or 'default'. Valid scope types: 'user' (email), 'group' (group email), 'domain' (domain name), 'default' (public access). Examples: 'user:john@example.com', 'group:team@example.com', 'domain:example.com', 'default'. Note: 'me' is NOT valid; use actual email/domain. The rule must exist - use GOOGLECALENDAR_LIST_ACL_RULES to find valid IDs.

requiredInputs.name: calendar_id
requiredInputs.description: Calendar identifier. To retrieve calendar IDs call the calendarList.list method. If you want to access the primary calendar of the currently logged in user, use the "primary" keyword.

outputs.name: data
outputs.description: Data from the action execution

outputs.name: error
outputs.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

outputs.name: successful
outputs.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

name: Create ACL Rule
description: Creates an access control rule for a calendar. Use when you need to grant sharing permissions to a user, group, or domain.

inputs.name: role
inputs.description: The role assigned to the scope. Possible values: "none" - Provides no access; "freeBusyReader" - Provides read access to free/busy information; "reader" - Provides read access to the calendar; "writer" - Provides read and write access to the calendar; "owner" - Provides ownership of the calendar.

inputs.name: scope
inputs.description: The extent to which calendar access is granted by this ACL rule. Specifies who gets the access (user, group, domain, or default).

inputs.name: type
inputs.description: The type of the scope. Possible values: "default" - The public scope (any user); "user" - Limits the scope to a single user; "group" - Limits the scope to a group; "domain" - Limits the scope to a domain.

inputs.name: value
inputs.description: The email address of a user or group, or the name of a domain, depending on the scope type. Omitted for type 'default'. For type 'user' or 'group', must be a valid email address (e.g., 'user@example.com'). For type 'domain', must be a domain name (e.g., 'example.com'). The value 'me' is NOT a valid email - use the actual email address of the user.

inputs.name: calendar_id
inputs.description: Calendar identifier. To retrieve calendar IDs call the calendarList.list method. If you want to access the primary calendar of the currently logged in user, use the "primary" keyword.

inputs.name: send_notifications
inputs.description: Whether to send notifications about the calendar sharing change. Optional. The default is true.

requiredInputs.name: role
requiredInputs.description: The role assigned to the scope. Possible values: "none" - Provides no access; "freeBusyReader" - Provides read access to free/busy information; "reader" - Provides read access to the calendar; "writer" - Provides read and write access to the calendar; "owner" - Provides ownership of the calendar.

requiredInputs.name: scope
requiredInputs.description: The extent to which calendar access is granted by this ACL rule. Specifies who gets the access (user, group, domain, or default).

requiredInputs.name: type
requiredInputs.description: The type of the scope. Possible values: "default" - The public scope (any user); "user" - Limits the scope to a single user; "group" - Limits the scope to a group; "domain" - Limits the scope to a domain.

requiredInputs.name: calendar_id
requiredInputs.description: Calendar identifier. To retrieve calendar IDs call the calendarList.list method. If you want to access the primary calendar of the currently logged in user, use the "primary" keyword.

optionalInputs.name: value
optionalInputs.description: The email address of a user or group, or the name of a domain, depending on the scope type. Omitted for type 'default'. For type 'user' or 'group', must be a valid email address (e.g., 'user@example.com'). For type 'domain', must be a domain name (e.g., 'example.com'). The value 'me' is NOT a valid email - use the actual email address of the user.

optionalInputs.name: send_notifications
optionalInputs.description: Whether to send notifications about the calendar sharing change. Optional. The default is true.

outputs.name: data
outputs.description: Data from the action execution

outputs.name: error
outputs.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

outputs.name: successful
outputs.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

name: List ACL Rules
description: Retrieves the list of access control rules (ACLs) for a specified calendar, providing the necessary 'rule_id' values required for updating specific ACL rules.

inputs.name: pageToken
inputs.description: Token specifying which result page to return. Optional.

inputs.name: syncToken
inputs.description: Token obtained from the nextSyncToken field returned on the last page of a previous list operation. It makes the result of this list operation contain only entries that have changed since then. Optional. The default is to retrieve all entries.

inputs.name: calendarId
inputs.description: Calendar identifier. To retrieve calendar IDs call the calendarList.list method. If you want to access the primary calendar of the currently logged in user, use the "primary" keyword.

inputs.name: maxResults
inputs.description: Maximum number of entries returned on one result page. Optional. The default is 100.

inputs.name: showDeleted
inputs.description: Whether to include deleted ACLs in the result. Optional. The default is False.

requiredInputs.name: calendarId
requiredInputs.description: Calendar identifier. To retrieve calendar IDs call the calendarList.list method. If you want to access the primary calendar of the currently logged in user, use the "primary" keyword.

optionalInputs.name: pageToken
optionalInputs.description: Token specifying which result page to return. Optional.

optionalInputs.name: syncToken
optionalInputs.description: Token obtained from the nextSyncToken field returned on the last page of a previous list operation. It makes the result of this list operation contain only entries that have changed since then. Optional. The default is to retrieve all entries.

optionalInputs.name: maxResults
optionalInputs.description: Maximum number of entries returned on one result page. Optional. The default is 100.

optionalInputs.name: showDeleted
optionalInputs.description: Whether to include deleted ACLs in the result. Optional. The default is False.

outputs.name: data
outputs.description: Data from the action execution

outputs.name: error
outputs.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

outputs.name: successful
outputs.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

name: Patch ACL Rule
description: Updates an existing access control rule for a calendar using patch semantics (partial update). This allows modifying specific fields without affecting other properties. IMPORTANT: The ACL rule must already exist on the calendar. This action cannot create new rules. If you receive a 404 Not Found error, the rule does not exist - use ACL insert to create it first, or use ACL list to verify available rules. Each patch request consumes three quota units. For domain-type ACL rules, if PATCH fails with 500 error, this action will automatically fallback to UPDATE method.

inputs.name: role
inputs.description: The role assigned to the scope. Possible values are: "none" - Provides no access; "freeBusyReader" - Provides read access to free/busy information; "reader" - Provides read access to the calendar (private events appear but details are hidden); "writer" - Provides read and write access to the calendar (private events and details are visible); "owner" - Provides ownership of the calendar (all permissions of writer plus ability to see and manipulate ACLs).

inputs.name: scope
inputs.description: The extent to which calendar access is granted by this ACL rule. Optional for patch operations. Must include `type` (one of: 'user', 'group', 'domain', 'default') and `value` (email address or domain name) for all types except 'default'.

inputs.name: type
inputs.description: The type of the scope. Possible values are: "default" - The public scope; "user" - Limits the scope to a single user; "group" - Limits the scope to a group; "domain" - Limits the scope to a domain.

inputs.name: value
inputs.description: The email address of a user or group, or the name of a domain, depending on the scope type. Omitted for type 'default'.

inputs.name: rule_id
inputs.description: ACL rule identifier of an existing rule. IMPORTANT: The rule must already exist on the calendar - this action cannot create new rules, only modify existing ones. Use the ACL list action to find existing rule IDs, or use the ACL insert action to create a new rule first. Format: 'type:value', such as 'user:email@example.com' or 'group:group@example.com'.

inputs.name: calendar_id
inputs.description: Calendar identifier. To retrieve calendar IDs call the calendarList.list method. If you want to access the primary calendar of the currently logged in user, use the "primary" keyword.

inputs.name: send_notifications
inputs.description: Whether to send notifications about the calendar sharing change. Note that there are no notifications on access removal. Optional. The default is True.

requiredInputs.name: rule_id
requiredInputs.description: ACL rule identifier of an existing rule. IMPORTANT: The rule must already exist on the calendar - this action cannot create new rules, only modify existing ones. Use the ACL list action to find existing rule IDs, or use the ACL insert action to create a new rule first. Format: 'type:value', such as 'user:email@example.com' or 'group:group@example.com'.

requiredInputs.name: calendar_id
requiredInputs.description: Calendar identifier. To retrieve calendar IDs call the calendarList.list method. If you want to access the primary calendar of the currently logged in user, use the "primary" keyword.

optionalInputs.name: role
optionalInputs.description: The role assigned to the scope. Possible values are: "none" - Provides no access; "freeBusyReader" - Provides read access to free/busy information; "reader" - Provides read access to the calendar (private events appear but details are hidden); "writer" - Provides read and write access to the calendar (private events and details are visible); "owner" - Provides ownership of the calendar (all permissions of writer plus ability to see and manipulate ACLs).

optionalInputs.name: scope
optionalInputs.description: The extent to which calendar access is granted by this ACL rule. Optional for patch operations. Must include `type` (one of: 'user', 'group', 'domain', 'default') and `value` (email address or domain name) for all types except 'default'.

optionalInputs.name: type
optionalInputs.description: The type of the scope. Possible values are: "default" - The public scope; "user" - Limits the scope to a single user; "group" - Limits the scope to a group; "domain" - Limits the scope to a domain.

optionalInputs.name: value
optionalInputs.description: The email address of a user or group, or the name of a domain, depending on the scope type. Omitted for type 'default'.

optionalInputs.name: send_notifications
optionalInputs.description: Whether to send notifications about the calendar sharing change. Note that there are no notifications on access removal. Optional. The default is True.

name: Update ACL Rule
description: Updates an access control rule for the specified calendar.

inputs.name: role
inputs.description:
The role assigned to the scope. Possible values are: 
- "none" - Provides no access. 
- "freeBusyReader" - Provides read access to free/busy information. 
- "reader" - Provides read access to the calendar. Private events will appear to users with reader access, but event details will be hidden. 
- "writer" - Provides read and write access to the calendar. Private events will appear to users with writer access, and event details will be visible. 
- "owner" - Provides ownership of the calendar. This role has all of the permissions of the writer role with the additional ability to see and manipulate ACLs.

inputs.name: rule_id
inputs.description: ACL rule identifier.

inputs.name: calendar_id
inputs.description: Calendar identifier. To retrieve calendar IDs call the calendarList.list method. If you want to access the primary calendar of the currently logged in user, use the "primary" keyword.

inputs.name: send_notifications
inputs.description: Whether to send notifications about the calendar sharing change. Note that there are no notifications on access removal. Optional. The default is True.

requiredInputs.name: role
requiredInputs.description:
The role assigned to the scope. Possible values are: 
- "none" - Provides no access. 
- "freeBusyReader" - Provides read access to free/busy information. 
- "reader" - Provides read access to the calendar. Private events will appear to users with reader access, but event details will be hidden. 
- "writer" - Provides read and write access to the calendar. Private events will appear to users with writer access, and event details will be visible. 
- "owner" - Provides ownership of the calendar. This role has all of the permissions of the writer role with the additional ability to see and manipulate ACLs.

requiredInputs.name: rule_id
requiredInputs.description: ACL rule identifier.

requiredInputs.name: calendar_id
requiredInputs.description: Calendar identifier. To retrieve calendar IDs call the calendarList.list method. If you want to access the primary calendar of the currently logged in user, use the "primary" keyword.

name: Watch ACL Changes
description: Tool to watch for changes to ACL resources. Use when you need to set up real-time notifications for access control list modifications on a calendar.

inputs.name: id
inputs.description: A UUID or similar unique string that identifies this channel.

inputs.name: type
inputs.description: The type of delivery mechanism used for this channel. Valid values are "web_hook" or "webhook".

inputs.name: token
inputs.description: An arbitrary string delivered to the target address with each notification delivered over this channel. Optional.

inputs.name: params
inputs.description: Additional parameters controlling delivery channel behavior. Optional.

inputs.name: ttl
inputs.description: Time-to-live in seconds for the notification channel. Default is 604800 seconds (one week).

inputs.name: address
inputs.description: The address where notifications are delivered for this channel.

inputs.description: Calendar identifier. To retrieve calendar IDs call the calendarList.list method. If you want to access the primary calendar of the currently logged in user, use the "primary" keyword.

name: Add file sharing preference (Deprecated)
description: DEPRECATED: Use GOOGLEDRIVE_CREATE_PERMISSION instead; use GOOGLEDRIVE_UPDATE_PERMISSION to modify existing permissions (avoids duplicate entries). Modifies sharing permissions for an existing Google Drive file, granting a specified role to a user, group, domain, or 'anyone'. Bulk calls may trigger 403 rateLimitExceeded (~100 req/100s/user); use jittered exponential backoff.

inputs.name: role
inputs.description: Permission role to grant. Accepted values: 'reader', 'commenter', 'writer', 'fileOrganizer', 'organizer', 'owner'. Invalid strings cause validation failures.

inputs.name: type
inputs.description: Type of grantee for the permission. Using 'anyone' with 'writer' or 'owner' broadly exposes the document — confirm before applying. Admin policies may block 'anyone' or domain-wide sharing. For type='anyone' with role='reader', the link must be explicitly shared; files are not publicly searchable.

inputs.name: domain
inputs.description: Domain to grant permission to (e.g., 'example.com'). Required if 'type' is 'domain'.

inputs.name: file_id
inputs.description: Unique identifier of the file to update sharing settings for. Must be an alphanumeric string containing only letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores (no slashes, spaces, or other special characters). Use GOOGLEDRIVE_FIND_FILE or GOOGLEDRIVE_LIST_FILES to get valid file IDs from your Google Drive. For shared drive membership, supply the shared drive ID, not an individual document ID.

inputs.name: email_address
inputs.description: Email address of the user or group. Required if 'type' is 'user' or 'group'.

inputs.name: transfer_ownership
inputs.description: Whether to transfer ownership to the specified user. Required when role is 'owner'. Only a single user can be specified in the request when transferring ownership. Ownership transfer is difficult to reverse — obtain explicit confirmation before setting true.

requiredInputs.name: role
requiredInputs.description: Permission role to grant. Accepted values: 'reader', 'commenter', 'writer', 'fileOrganizer', 'organizer', 'owner'. Invalid strings cause validation failures.

requiredInputs.name: type
requiredInputs.description: Type of grantee for the permission. Using 'anyone' with 'writer' or 'owner' broadly exposes the document — confirm before applying. Admin policies may block 'anyone' or domain-wide sharing. For type='anyone' with role='reader', the link must be explicitly shared; files are not publicly searchable.

requiredInputs.name: file_id
requiredInputs.description: Unique identifier of the file to update sharing settings for. Must be an alphanumeric string containing only letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores (no slashes, spaces, or other special characters). Use GOOGLEDRIVE_FIND_FILE or GOOGLEDRIVE_LIST_FILES to get valid file IDs from your Google Drive. For shared drive membership, supply the shared drive ID, not an individual document ID.

optionalInputs.name: domain
optionalInputs.description: Domain to grant permission to (e.g., 'example.com'). Required if 'type' is 'domain'.

optionalInputs.name: email_address
optionalInputs.description: Email address of the user or group. Required if 'type' is 'user' or 'group'.

optionalInputs.name: transfer_ownership
optionalInputs.description: Whether to transfer ownership to the specified user. Required when role is 'owner'. Only a single user can be specified in the request when transferring ownership. Ownership transfer is difficult to reverse — obtain explicit confirmation before setting true.

outputs.name: data
outputs.description: Data from the action execution

outputs.name: error
outputs.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

outputs.name: successful
outputs.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

name: Modify email labels
description: Adds and/or removes specified Gmail labels for a message; ensure `message_id` and all `label_ids` are valid (use 'listLabels' for custom label IDs).

inputs.name: user_id
inputs.description: User's email address or 'me' for the authenticated user.

inputs.name: message_id
inputs.description: Immutable ID of the message to modify. Gmail message IDs are 15-16 character hexadecimal strings (e.g., '1a2b3c4d5e6f7890'). IMPORTANT: Do NOT use UUIDs (32-character strings like '093ca4662b214d5eba8f4ceeaad63433'), thread IDs, or internal system IDs - these will cause 'Invalid id value' errors. Obtain valid message IDs from: (1) 'GMAIL_FETCH_EMAILS' response 'messageId' field, (2) 'GMAIL_FETCH_MESSAGE_BY_THREAD_ID' response, or (3) 'GMAIL_LIST_THREADS' and then fetching thread messages.

inputs.name: add_label_ids
inputs.description: Label IDs to add (IDs, not display names). System labels: INBOX, SPAM, TRASH, UNREAD, STARRED, IMPORTANT, CATEGORY_PERSONAL, CATEGORY_SOCIAL, CATEGORY_PROMOTIONS, CATEGORY_UPDATES, CATEGORY_FORUMS. Use full CATEGORY_ prefix (e.g., 'CATEGORY_UPDATES' not 'UPDATES'). Custom labels: call 'listLabels' first to get the ID (format: 'Label_<number>'). Do NOT use the label display name; the API requires the ID. SENT, DRAFT, CHAT are immutable and cannot be added or removed. A label cannot appear in both add_label_ids and remove_label_ids.

inputs.name: remove_label_ids
inputs.description: Label IDs to remove (IDs, not display names). System labels: INBOX, SPAM, TRASH, UNREAD, STARRED, IMPORTANT, CATEGORY_PERSONAL, CATEGORY_SOCIAL, CATEGORY_PROMOTIONS, CATEGORY_UPDATES, CATEGORY_FORUMS. Use full CATEGORY_ prefix (e.g., 'CATEGORY_UPDATES' not 'UPDATES'). Custom labels: call 'listLabels' first to get the ID (format: 'Label_<number>'). SENT, DRAFT, CHAT are immutable and cannot be removed. Common operations: to mark as read, remove 'UNREAD'; to archive, remove 'INBOX'. A label cannot appear in both add_label_ids and remove_label_ids.

requiredInputs.name: message_id
requiredInputs.description: Immutable ID of the message to modify. Gmail message IDs are 15-16 character hexadecimal strings (e.g., '1a2b3c4d5e6f7890'). IMPORTANT: Do NOT use UUIDs (32-character strings like '093ca4662b214d5eba8f4ceeaad63433'), thread IDs, or internal system IDs - these will cause 'Invalid id value' errors. Obtain valid message IDs from: (1) 'GMAIL_FETCH_EMAILS' response 'messageId' field, (2) 'GMAIL_FETCH_MESSAGE_BY_THREAD_ID' response, or (3) 'GMAIL_LIST_THREADS' and then fetching thread messages.

optionalInputs.name: user_id
optionalInputs.description: User's email address or 'me' for the authenticated user.

optionalInputs.name: add_label_ids
optionalInputs.description: Label IDs to add (IDs, not display names). System labels: INBOX, SPAM, TRASH, UNREAD, STARRED, IMPORTANT, CATEGORY_PERSONAL, CATEGORY_SOCIAL, CATEGORY_PROMOTIONS, CATEGORY_UPDATES, CATEGORY_FORUMS. Use full CATEGORY_ prefix (e.g., 'CATEGORY_UPDATES' not 'UPDATES'). Custom labels: call 'listLabels' first to get the ID (format: 'Label_<number>'). Do NOT use the label display name; the API requires the ID. SENT, DRAFT, CHAT are immutable and cannot be added or removed. A label cannot appear in both add_label_ids and remove_label_ids.

optionalInputs.name: remove_label_ids
optionalInputs.description: Label IDs to remove (IDs, not display names). System labels: INBOX, SPAM, TRASH, UNREAD, STARRED, IMPORTANT, CATEGORY_PERSONAL, CATEGORY_SOCIAL, CATEGORY_PROMOTIONS, CATEGORY_UPDATES, CATEGORY_FORUMS. Use full CATEGORY_ prefix (e.g., 'CATEGORY_UPDATES' not 'UPDATES'). Custom labels: call 'listLabels' first to get the ID (format: 'Label_<number>'). SENT, DRAFT, CHAT are immutable and cannot be removed. Common operations: to mark as read, remove 'UNREAD'; to archive, remove 'INBOX'. A label cannot appear in both add_label_ids and remove_label_ids.

inputs.name: resource_name

name: Add Sheet to Existing Spreadsheet
description: Adds a new sheet to a spreadsheet. Supports three sheet types: GRID, OBJECT, and DATA_SOURCE. SHEET TYPES: - GRID (default): Standard spreadsheet with rows/columns. Use properties to set dimensions, tab color, etc. - OBJECT: Sheet containing a chart. Requires objectSheetConfig with chartSpec (basicChart or pieChart). - DATA_SOURCE: Sheet connected to BigQuery. Requires dataSourceConfig with bigQuery spec and bigquery.readonly OAuth scope. OTHER NOTES: - Sheet names must be unique; use forceUnique=true to auto-append suffix (_2, _3) if name exists - For tab colors, use EITHER rgbColor OR themeColor, not both - Avoid 'index' when creating sheets in parallel (causes errors) - OBJECT sheets are created via addChart with position.newSheet=true - DATA_SOURCE sheets require bigquery.readonly OAuth scope Use cases: Add standard grid sheet, create chart on dedicated sheet, connect to BigQuery data source.

inputs.name: title
inputs.description: The name for the new sheet tab. Must be unique within the spreadsheet. Example: "Q3 Report", "Sales Data 2025". This is a convenience parameter - alternatively, you can set this via properties.title. Note: sheet_name is also accepted as an alias for title.

inputs.name: properties
inputs.description: Advanced sheet properties (grid dimensions, tab color, position, etc.). For simple cases, just use the 'title' parameter directly. Use this for additional customization.

inputs.name: index
inputs.description: The zero-based index where the sheet should be inserted. Must be less than or equal to the current number of sheets. If not set, the sheet will be added at the end. Example: 0 for the first position. CONCURRENCY WARNING: Do not use 'index' when creating multiple sheets in parallel - this causes 'index is too high' errors. For parallel creation, omit this field and let sheets be added at the end.

inputs.name: title
inputs.description: The name of the sheet. Must be unique within the spreadsheet. Example: "Q3 Report", "Sales Data 2025"

inputs.name: hidden
inputs.description: True if the sheet is hidden in the UI, false if it's visible.

inputs.name: sheetId
inputs.description: The ID of the sheet. If not set, an ID will be randomly generated. Must be non-negative and unique within the spreadsheet. WARNING: Avoid setting this unless you need a specific ID.

inputs.name: sheetType
inputs.description:
Sheet type enum for AddSheetRequest.

IMPORTANT: AddSheetRequest only supports creating GRID sheets.
- For OBJECT sheets: Use 'Create Chart' action with position.newSheet=true
- For DATA_SOURCE sheets: Use AddDataSourceRequest (requires extra scopes/permissions)

inputs.name: rightToLeft
inputs.description: True if the sheet is an RTL sheet, false if it's LTR.

inputs.name: tabColorStyle
inputs.description: The color of the sheet tab. Use EITHER rgbColor OR themeColor, never both.

inputs.name: rgbColor
inputs.description: RGB color. Use EITHER rgbColor OR themeColor, never both. If using rgbColor, provide values for red, green, blue (0.0-1.0).

inputs.name: red
inputs.description: The amount of red in the color as a value in the interval [0, 1].

inputs.name: blue
inputs.description: The amount of blue in the color as a value in the interval [0, 1].

inputs.name: alpha
inputs.description: The fraction of this color that should be applied to the pixel. E.g. 0.5 for 50% transparent.

inputs.name: green
inputs.description: The amount of green in the color as a value in the interval [0, 1].

inputs.name: themeColor
inputs.description: Theme color. Use EITHER themeColor OR rgbColor, never both. Use predefined theme colors like ACCENT1, TEXT, BACKGROUND, etc.

inputs.name: gridProperties
inputs.description: Additional properties of the sheet if it's a grid sheet.

inputs.name: rowCount
inputs.description: The number of rows in the sheet. Defaults to 100 rows if not specified (to conserve cell quota). Google Sheets has a 10M cell workbook limit.

inputs.name: columnCount
inputs.description: The number of columns in the sheet. Defaults to 26 columns if not specified. Google Sheets has a 10M cell workbook limit.

inputs.name: hideGridlines
inputs.description: True if the gridlines are hidden, false if they are shown.

inputs.name: frozenRowCount
inputs.description: The number of rows that are frozen in the sheet.

inputs.name: frozenColumnCount
inputs.description: The number of columns that are frozen in the sheet.

inputs.name: rowGroupControlAfter
inputs.description: True if the row group control toggle is shown after the group, false if before.

inputs.name: columnGroupControlAfter
inputs.description: True if the column group control toggle is shown after the group, false if before.

inputs.name: force_unique
inputs.description: When True (default), automatically ensures the sheet name is unique by appending a numeric suffix (e.g., '_2', '_3') if the requested name already exists. This makes the action resilient to retries and parallel workflows. When False, the action fails with an error if a sheet with the same name already exists.

inputs.name: spreadsheet_id
inputs.description: REQUIRED. Cannot be empty. The ID of the target spreadsheet where the new sheet will be added. This is the long alphanumeric string in the Google Sheet URL (e.g., '1BxiMVs0XRA5nFMdKvBdBZjgmUUqptlbs74OgvE2upms'). Use 'Search Spreadsheets' action to find the spreadsheet ID by name if you don't have it.

inputs.name: data_source_config
inputs.description:
Configuration for creating a DATA_SOURCE sheet.

DATA_SOURCE sheets connect to external data sources like BigQuery.
The API uses addDataSource request which automatically creates the associated sheet.

IMPORTANT: Requires additional OAuth scope: bigquery.readonly

inputs.description: The data source specification (currently supports BigQuery).

inputs.name: bigQuery
inputs.description: BigQuery data source configuration. Requires the bigquery.readonly OAuth scope.

inputs.name: projectId
inputs.description: The ID of a BigQuery-enabled Google Cloud project with billing attached.

inputs.name: querySpec
inputs.description: Configuration for a BigQuery query-based data source.

inputs.name: rawQuery
inputs.description: The raw SQL query to execute in BigQuery.

inputs.name: tableSpec
inputs.description: Configuration for a BigQuery table-based data source.

inputs.name: tableId
inputs.description: The BigQuery table ID.

inputs.name: datasetId
inputs.description: The BigQuery dataset ID containing the table.

inputs.name: tableProjectId
inputs.description: The Google Cloud project ID containing the table (defaults to the spreadsheet's project).

inputs.name: object_sheet_config
inputs.description:

To create an OBJECT sheet, you must provide chart configuration.
The API uses addChart with position.newSheet=true to create the chart on its own sheet.

inputs.description: The end column (0-indexed, exclusive). Column B = 2.

inputs.description: The start column (0-indexed, inclusive). Column A = 0.

inputs.description: The axis this series maps to. Usually LEFT_AXIS or RIGHT_AXIS.

inputs.description: For stacked charts: NOT_STACKED, STACKED, or PERCENT_STACKED.

name: Aggregate Column Data
description: Searches for rows where a specific column matches a value and performs mathematical operations on data from another column.

inputs.name: operation
inputs.description: The mathematical operation to perform on the target column values.

inputs.name: sheet_name
inputs.description: The name of the specific sheet within the spreadsheet. Matching is case-insensitive. If no exact match is found, partial matches will be attempted (e.g., 'overview' will match 'Overview 2025').

inputs.name: search_value
inputs.description: The exact value to search for in the search column. Case-sensitive by default. If not provided (or if search_column is not provided), all rows in the target column will be aggregated without filtering.

inputs.name: search_column
inputs.description: The column to search in for filtering rows. Can be a letter (e.g., 'A', 'B') or column name from header row (e.g., 'Region', 'Department'). If not provided, all rows in the target column will be aggregated without filtering.

inputs.name: target_column
inputs.description: The column to aggregate data from. Can be a letter (e.g., 'C', 'D') or column name from header row (e.g., 'Sales', 'Revenue').

inputs.name: case_sensitive
inputs.description: Whether the search should be case-sensitive.

inputs.name: has_header_row
inputs.description: Whether the first row contains column headers. If True, column names can be used for search_column and target_column.

inputs.name: spreadsheet_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the Google Sheets spreadsheet.

inputs.name: percentage_total
inputs.description: For percentage operation, the total value to calculate percentage against. If not provided, uses sum of all values in target column.

inputs.name: additional_filters
inputs.description: Extra column=value conditions applied with AND logic on top of search_column/search_value. Use this to filter on multiple columns simultaneously. Example: [{"column": "Region", "value": "APAC"}] combined with search_column=Product/search_value=Beacon returns only rows where Product=Beacon AND Region=APAC.

inputs.name: value
inputs.description: Exact value to match in the column.

inputs.name: column
inputs.description: Column letter (e.g., 'B') or header name (e.g., 'Region') to filter on.

requiredInputs.name: operation
requiredInputs.description: The mathematical operation to perform on the target column values.

requiredInputs.name: sheet_name

name: Archive Custom Dimension
description: Tool to archive a CustomDimension on a property. Use when you need to remove a custom dimension from active use without permanently deleting it. Archived dimensions cannot be used in new reports.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: Required. The resource name of the CustomDimension to archive. Must be in the exact format: properties/{property_id}/customDimensions/{dimension_id} where property_id and dimension_id are numeric identifiers. IMPORTANT: Must start with 'properties/' prefix, contain '/customDimensions/' in the middle, have no trailing slashes, and no additional path segments. Valid example: 'properties/489591273/customDimensions/13661259421'. Invalid examples: '489591273/13661259421' (missing prefix), 'properties/489591273/customDimensions/13661259421/' (trailing slash), 'properties/489591273/customDimensions/13661259421/extra' (extra segments)

requiredInputs.name: name
requiredInputs.description: Required. The resource name of the CustomDimension to archive. Must be in the exact format: properties/{property_id}/customDimensions/{dimension_id} where property_id and dimension_id are numeric identifiers. IMPORTANT: Must start with 'properties/' prefix, contain '/customDimensions/' in the middle, have no trailing slashes, and no additional path segments. Valid example: 'properties/489591273/customDimensions/13661259421'. Invalid examples: '489591273/13661259421' (missing prefix), 'properties/489591273/customDimensions/13661259421/' (trailing slash), 'properties/489591273/customDimensions/13661259421/extra' (extra segments)

name: Autocomplete Place Predictions
description: Returns place and query predictions for text input. Use when implementing as-you-type autocomplete functionality for place searches. Returns up to five predictions ordered by relevance.

inputs.name: input
inputs.description: Text string to search for place predictions. Can be full words, substrings, place names, addresses, or plus codes. Must be non-empty.

inputs.name: origin
inputs.description: Latitude/longitude point for distance calculations.

inputs.name: latitude
inputs.description: Latitude coordinate.

inputs.name: longitude
inputs.description: Longitude coordinate.

inputs.name: regionCode
inputs.description: Region code in ccTLD format for formatting responses (e.g., 'us', 'br').

inputs.name: inputOffset
inputs.description: Zero-based Unicode character offset of cursor position in the input string.

inputs.name: languageCode
inputs.description: Preferred language for results using IETF BCP-47 language codes (e.g., 'en-US', 'es-ES').

inputs.name: locationBias
inputs.description: Area to bias search results toward.

inputs.name: circle
inputs.description: Circular area for location bias or restriction.

inputs.name: center
inputs.description: Center point with latitude and longitude coordinates.

inputs.name: radius
inputs.description: Radius in meters (0.0-50000.0 for bias, must exceed 0.0 for restriction).

inputs.name: rectangle
inputs.description: Rectangular area for location bias or restriction.

inputs.name: low
inputs.description: Southwest corner with latitude and longitude coordinates.

inputs.name: high
inputs.description: Northeast corner with latitude and longitude coordinates.

inputs.name: sessionToken
inputs.description: User-generated string grouping calls into sessions for billing purposes.

inputs.name: includedRegionCodes
inputs.description: Up to 15 two-character country codes (ISO 3166-1 Alpha-2) to restrict results. Query predictions unavailable when this is set.

inputs.name: locationRestriction
inputs.description: Area to restrict search results within.

inputs.name: circle
inputs.description: Circular area for location bias or restriction.

inputs.name: center
inputs.description: Center point with latitude and longitude coordinates.

inputs.description: Radius in meters (0.0-50000.0 for bias, must exceed 0.0 for restriction).

inputs.description: Rectangular area for location bias or restriction.

inputs.name: low
inputs.description: Southwest corner with latitude and longitude coordinates.

inputs.name: high
inputs.description: Northeast corner with latitude and longitude coordinates.

inputs.name: includedPrimaryTypes
inputs.description: Restricts results to up to five specified primary types (e.g., 'restaurant', 'cafe').

inputs.name: includeQueryPredictions
inputs.description: Includes query predictions in response for text searches (default: false).

inputs.name: includePureServiceAreaBusinesses
inputs.description: Includes businesses without physical locations (default: false).

name: Auto-Resize Rows or Columns
description: Auto-fit column widths or row heights for a dimension range using batchUpdate.autoResizeDimensions. Use when you need to automatically adjust row heights or column widths to fit content after writing data.

inputs.name: sheet_id
inputs.description: The numeric ID of the sheet to resize. Either sheet_id or sheet_name must be provided. If both are provided, sheet_name takes precedence and will be resolved to sheet_id.

inputs.name: dimension
inputs.description: The dimension to auto-resize. Use 'ROWS' to auto-fit row heights or 'COLUMNS' to auto-fit column widths.

inputs.name: end_index
inputs.description: The zero-based end index of the dimension range to resize (exclusive). Must be greater than start_index. For example, to resize columns A-C, use start_index=0 and end_index=3.

inputs.name: sheet_name
inputs.description: The name of the sheet to resize. Either sheet_id or sheet_name must be provided. Using sheet_name is recommended as it's more intuitive. If both sheet_id and sheet_name are provided, sheet_name takes precedence.

inputs.name: start_index
inputs.description: The zero-based start index of the dimension range to resize (inclusive). For columns, 0 = column A. For rows, 0 = row 1.

inputs.name: spreadsheet_id
inputs.description: The ID of the spreadsheet containing the sheet to resize.

requiredInputs.name: dimension
requiredInputs.description: The dimension to auto-resize. Use 'ROWS' to auto-fit row heights or 'COLUMNS' to auto-fit column widths.

requiredInputs.name: end_index

requiredInputs.name: spreadsheet_id

name: Batch Clear Values By Data Filter
description: Clears one or more ranges of values from a spreadsheet using data filters. The caller must specify the spreadsheet ID and one or more DataFilters. Ranges matching any of the specified data filters will be cleared. Only values are cleared -- all other properties of the cell (such as formatting, data validation, etc..) are kept.

inputs.name: dataFilters
inputs.description: The DataFilters used to determine which ranges to clear.

inputs.name: a1Range
inputs.description: Selects data that matches the specified A1 range.

inputs.name: gridRange
inputs.description: Selects data that matches the range described by the GridRange.

inputs.name: sheetId
inputs.description: The sheet this range is on.

inputs.name: endRowIndex
inputs.description: The end row (exclusive) of the range, or not set if unbounded.

inputs.name: startRowIndex
inputs.description: The start row (inclusive) of the range, or not set if unbounded.

inputs.name: endColumnIndex
inputs.description: The end column (exclusive) of the range, or not set if unbounded.

inputs.name: startColumnIndex
inputs.description: The start column (inclusive) of the range, or not set if unbounded.

inputs.name: developerMetadataLookup
inputs.description: Selects data associated with the developer metadata matching the criteria described by this DeveloperMetadataLookup.

inputs.name: metadataId
inputs.description: Limits the selected developer metadata to that which has a matching DeveloperMetadata.metadata_id.

inputs.name: visibility
inputs.description: Limits the selected developer metadata to that which has a matching DeveloperMetadata.visibility. Valid values: DEVELOPER_METADATA_VISIBILITY_UNSPECIFIED, DOCUMENT, PROJECT.

inputs.name: metadataKey
inputs.description: Limits the selected developer metadata to that which has a matching DeveloperMetadata.metadata_key.

inputs.name: locationType
inputs.description: Limits the selected developer metadata to those entries which are associated with locations of the specified type. Valid values: DEVELOPER_METADATA_LOCATION_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED, ROW, COLUMN, SHEET, SPREADSHEET, ALL_METADATA_LOCATION.

inputs.name: metadataValue
inputs.description: Limits the selected developer metadata to that which has a matching DeveloperMetadata.metadata_value.

inputs.name: metadataLocation
inputs.description: Limits the selected developer metadata to those entries associated with the specified location.

inputs.name: sheetId
inputs.description: The ID of the sheet the metadata is associated with.

inputs.name: spreadsheet
inputs.description: True if the metadata is associated with the entire spreadsheet.

inputs.description: A grid range covering all spreadsheet, sheet, row, and column metadata that belong to the same unioned group.

name: Batch Create Media Items
description: Batch upload and create media items in Google Photos. Supports three input methods: 1. 'urls': Simple list of public URLs (file names extracted automatically) 2. 'media_files': List of objects with url/file, file_name, and description 3. 'files': List of FileUploadable objects for pre-uploaded files Media items can optionally be added to an album at a specific position. Maximum 50 items per request.

inputs.name: urls
inputs.description: Simplified input: List of public URLs of media files to upload. File names will be extracted from URLs automatically. Use 'media_files' for more control over file names and descriptions.

inputs.name: files
inputs.description: List of files to upload to Google Photos.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The filename that will be used when uploading the file to the destination service

inputs.name: s3key
inputs.description: The S3 key of a publicly accessible file, typically returned from a previous download action that stored the file in S3. This key references an existing file that can be uploaded to another service.

inputs.name: mimetype
inputs.description: The MIME type of the file

inputs.name: albumId
inputs.description: Optional identifier of the album where the media items are to be added. If not specified, media items are added to the user's library only.

inputs.name: media_files
inputs.description: List of media files to upload. Each item can specify either a 'url' (public URL) or a 'file' (FileUploadable object). Maximum 50 items per request.

inputs.name: url
inputs.description: Public URL of the media file to upload (must be directly accessible). The action will download the file from this URL and upload it to Google Photos.

inputs.name: file
inputs.description: Alternative to 'url'.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The filename that will be used when uploading the file to the destination service

inputs.name: s3key
inputs.description: The S3 key of a publicly accessible file, typically returned from a previous download action that stored the file in S3. This key references an existing file that can be uploaded to another service.

inputs.name: mimetype
inputs.description: The MIME type of the file

inputs.name: file_name
inputs.description: File name for the uploaded media when using URL. Required when URL is provided. Should include the file extension (e.g., 'photo.jpg', 'video.mp4').

inputs.name: description
inputs.description: Optional description for this media item

inputs.name: albumPosition
inputs.description: Position in the album to add the media items.

inputs.name: position
inputs.description: The position in the album where the media items should be added.

inputs.name: relativeMediaItemId
inputs.description: The media item to which the position is relative.

inputs.name: relativeEnrichmentItemId
inputs.description: The enrichment item to which the position is relative.

optionalInputs.name: urls
optionalInputs.description: Simplified input: List of public URLs of media files to upload. File names will be extracted from URLs automatically. Use 'media_files' for more control over file names and descriptions.

optionalInputs.name: files

name: Batch get spreadsheet
description: Retrieves data from specified cell ranges in a Google Spreadsheet.

inputs.name: ranges
inputs.description: A list of cell ranges in A1 notation from which to retrieve data. If this list is omitted, empty, or contains only empty strings, all data from the first sheet of the spreadsheet will be fetched. Empty strings in the list are automatically filtered out. Supported formats: (1) Bare sheet name like 'Sheet1' to get all data from that sheet, (2) Sheet with range like 'Sheet1!A1:B2', (3) Just cell reference like 'A1:B2' (uses first sheet). For sheet names with spaces or special characters, enclose in single quotes (e.g., "'My Sheet'" or "'My Sheet'!A1:B2"). IMPORTANT: For large sheets, always use bounded ranges with explicit row limits (e.g., 'Sheet1!A1:Z10000' instead of 'Sheet1!A:Z'). Unbounded column ranges like 'A:Z' on sheets with >10,000 rows may cause timeouts or errors. If you need all data from a large sheet, fetch in chunks of 10,000 rows at a time.

inputs.name: majorDimension
inputs.description: The major dimension for organizing data in results.

inputs.name: spreadsheet_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the Google Spreadsheet from which data will be retrieved. This is the ID found in the spreadsheet URL after /d/. You can provide either the spreadsheet ID directly or a full Google Sheets URL (the ID will be extracted automatically).

inputs.name: valueRenderOption
inputs.description: How values should be rendered in the output. FORMATTED_VALUE: Values are calculated and formatted (default). UNFORMATTED_VALUE: Values are calculated but not formatted. FORMULA: Values are not calculated; the formula is returned instead.

inputs.name: dateTimeRenderOption
inputs.description: How dates and times should be rendered in the output. SERIAL_NUMBER: Dates are returned as serial numbers (default). FORMATTED_STRING: Dates returned as formatted strings.

inputs.name: empty_strings_filtered
inputs.description: Indicates whether empty strings were filtered from the response.

requiredInputs.name: spreadsheet_id
requiredInputs.description: The unique identifier of the Google Spreadsheet from which data will be retrieved. This is the ID found in the spreadsheet URL after /d/. You can provide either the spreadsheet ID directly or a full Google Sheets URL (the ID will be extracted automatically).

optionalInputs.name: ranges
optionalInputs.description: A list of cell ranges in A1 notation from which to retrieve data. If this list is omitted, empty, or contains only empty strings, all data from the first sheet of the spreadsheet will be fetched. Empty strings in the list are automatically filtered out. Supported formats: (1) Bare sheet name like 'Sheet1' to get all data from that sheet, (2) Sheet with range like 'Sheet1!A1:B2', (3) Just cell reference like 'A1:B2' (uses first sheet). For sheet names with spaces or special characters, enclose in single quotes (e.g., "'My Sheet'" or "'My Sheet'!A1:B2"). IMPORTANT: For large sheets, always use bounded ranges with explicit row limits (e.g., 'Sheet1!A1:Z10000' instead of 'Sheet1!A:Z'). Unbounded column ranges like 'A:Z' on sheets with >10,000 rows may cause timeouts or errors. If you need all data from a large sheet, fetch in chunks of 10,000 rows at a time.

name: Batch modify Gmail messages
description: Modify labels on multiple Gmail messages in one efficient API call. Supports up to 1,000 messages per request for bulk operations like archiving, marking as read/unread, or applying custom labels. High-volume calls may return 429 rateLimitExceeded or 403 userRateLimitExceeded; apply exponential backoff.

inputs.name: userId
inputs.description: User's email address or 'me' for the authenticated user.

inputs.name: messageIds
inputs.description: List of message IDs to modify. Maximum 1,000 message IDs per request. Get message IDs from GMAIL_FETCH_EMAILS or GMAIL_LIST_THREADS actions. Accepts 'messageIds', 'ids', or 'message_ids' as the parameter name.

inputs.name: addLabelIds
inputs.description: List of label IDs to add to the messages. IMPORTANT: Use label IDs, NOT label display names. System labels use their name as ID: INBOX, STARRED, IMPORTANT, SENT, DRAFT, SPAM, TRASH, UNREAD, CATEGORY_PERSONAL, CATEGORY_SOCIAL, CATEGORY_PROMOTIONS, CATEGORY_UPDATES, CATEGORY_FORUMS. Custom labels MUST use their ID (format: 'Label_XXX', e.g., 'Label_1', 'Label_25'), NOT the display name (e.g., do NOT use 'Work' or 'Projects'). Call GMAIL_LIST_LABELS first to get the 'id' field for custom labels. At least one of add_label_ids or remove_label_ids must be provided. CONSTRAINT: Label IDs must NOT overlap with remove_label_ids - cannot add and remove the same label.

inputs.name: removeLabelIds
inputs.description: List of label IDs to remove from the messages. IMPORTANT: Use label IDs, NOT label display names. System labels use their name as ID: INBOX, STARRED, IMPORTANT, SENT, SPAM, TRASH, UNREAD. Custom labels MUST use their ID (format: 'Label_XXX', e.g., 'Label_1', 'Label_25'), NOT the display name (e.g., do NOT use 'Work' or 'Projects'). Call GMAIL_LIST_LABELS first to get the 'id' field for custom labels. Common use cases: Remove 'UNREAD' to mark as read, remove 'INBOX' to archive. Note: 'DRAFT' cannot be removed - use GMAIL_DELETE_DRAFT instead. At least one of add_label_ids or remove_label_ids must be provided. CONSTRAINT: Label IDs must NOT overlap with add_label_ids - cannot add and remove the same label.

requiredInputs.name: messageIds
requiredInputs.description: List of message IDs to modify. Maximum 1,000 message IDs per request. Get message IDs from GMAIL_FETCH_EMAILS or GMAIL_LIST_THREADS actions. Accepts 'messageIds', 'ids', or 'message_ids' as the parameter name.

optionalInputs.name: userId
optionalInputs.description: User's email address or 'me' for the authenticated user.

Custom labels MUST use their ID (format: 'Label_XXX', e.g., 'Label_1', 'Label_25'), NOT the display name (e.g., do NOT use 'Work' or 'Projects'). Call GMAIL_LIST_LABELS first to get the 'id' field for custom labels. At least one of add_label_ids or remove_label_ids must be provided. CONSTRAINT: Label IDs must NOT overlap with remove_label_ids - cannot add and remove the same label.

optionalInputs.name: removeLabelIds
optionalInputs.description: List of label IDs to remove from the messages. IMPORTANT: Use label IDs, NOT label display names. System labels use their name as ID: INBOX, STARRED, IMPORTANT, SENT, SPAM, TRASH, UNREAD. Custom labels MUST use their ID (format: 'Label_XXX', e.g., 'Label_1', 'Label_25'), NOT the display name (e.g., do NOT use 'Work' or 'Projects'). name: Batch Run Pivot Reports
description: Tool to return multiple pivot reports in a batch for a GA4 property. Use when you need to fetch multiple pivot table reports with multi-dimensional analysis in a single request.

inputs.name: property
inputs.description: Required. The GA4 property resource name. Format: properties/{property_id}

inputs.name: requests
inputs.description: Required. Up to 5 individual RunPivotReportRequest objects. Each request can have dimensions, metrics, dateRanges, pivots, dimensionFilter, metricFilter, currencyCode, cohortSpec, keepEmptyRows, returnPropertyQuota, and comparisons fields. CRITICAL CONSTRAINT: Every dimension defined in 'dimensions' MUST be used in at least one of: pivots (fieldNames), dimensionFilter, or orderBys. Dimensions not used anywhere will cause a 400 error. The only exception is 'dateRange' dimensions. Additionally, all fieldNames in pivots must reference dimensions defined in the request's 'dimensions' array, and no two pivots can share the same dimension.

name: Batch update spreadsheet (Deprecated)
description: DEPRECATED: Use GOOGLESHEETS_VALUES_UPDATE instead. Write values to ONE range in a Google Sheet, or append as new rows if no start cell is given. IMPORTANT - This tool does NOT accept the Google Sheets API's native batch format: - WRONG: {"data": [{"range": "...", "values": [[...]]}], ...} - CORRECT: {"sheet_name": "...", "values": [[...]], "first_cell_location": "...", ...} To update MULTIPLE ranges, make SEPARATE CALLS to this tool for each range. Features: - Auto-expands grid for large datasets (prevents range errors) - Set first_cell_location to write at a specific position (e.g., "A1", "B5") - Omit first_cell_location to append values as new rows at the end Requirements: Target sheet must exist and spreadsheet must contain at least one worksheet.

inputs.name: values
inputs.description: A 2D array of cell values where each inner array represents a row. Values can be strings, numbers, booleans, or None/null for empty cells. Ensure columns are properly aligned across rows.

inputs.name: sheet_name
inputs.description: The name of the specific sheet (tab) within the spreadsheet to update (required, separate from cell reference). Case-insensitive matching is supported (e.g., 'sheet1' will match 'Sheet1'). Note: Default sheet names are locale-dependent (e.g., 'Sheet1' in English, 'Foglio1' in Italian, 'Hoja 1' in Spanish, '시트1' in Korean, 'Feuille 1' in French). If you specify a common default name like 'Sheet1' and it doesn't exist, the action will automatically use the first sheet in the spreadsheet.

inputs.name: spreadsheet_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the Google Sheets spreadsheet to be updated. Must be an alphanumeric string (with hyphens and underscores allowed) typically 44 characters long. Can be found in the spreadsheet URL between '/d/' and '/edit'. Example: 'https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1BxiMVs0XRA5nFMdKvBdBZjgmUUqptlbs74OgvE2upms/edit' has ID '1BxiMVs0XRA5nFMdKvBdBZjgmUUqptlbs74OgvE2upms'.

inputs.name: value_input_option
inputs.description: How input data should be interpreted. 'USER_ENTERED': Values are parsed as if typed by a user (e.g., strings may become numbers/dates, formulas are calculated). 'RAW': Values are stored exactly as provided without parsing (e.g., '123' stays as string, '=SUM(A1:B1)' is not calculated).

inputs.name: first_cell_location
inputs.description: The starting cell for the update range, specified as a single cell in A1 notation WITHOUT sheet prefix (e.g., 'A1', 'B2', 'AA931'). The update will extend from this cell to the right and down based on the provided values. Sheet name must be provided separately in the 'sheet_name' field. If omitted or set to null, values are appended as new rows to the sheet. Note: Use only a single cell reference (e.g., 'AA931'), NOT a range (e.g., 'AA931:AF931') or sheet-prefixed notation (e.g., 'Sheet1!A1').

inputs.name: include_values_in_response
inputs.description: If set to True, the response will include the updated values in the 'spreadsheet.responses[].updatedData' field. The updatedData object contains 'range' (A1 notation), 'majorDimension' (ROWS), and 'values' (2D array of the actual cell values after the update).

requiredInputs.name: values
requiredInputs.description: A 2D array of cell values where each inner array represents a row. Values can be strings, numbers, booleans, or None/null for empty cells. Ensure columns are properly aligned across rows.

requiredInputs.name: sheet_name
requiredInputs.description: The name of the specific sheet (tab) within the spreadsheet to update (required, separate from cell reference). Case-insensitive matching is supported (e.g., 'sheet1' will match 'Sheet1'). Note: Default sheet names are locale-dependent (e.g., 'Sheet1' in English, 'Foglio1' in Italian, 'Hoja 1' in Spanish, '시트1' in Korean, 'Feuille 1' in French). inputs.description: The new values to apply to the spreadsheet. If more than one range is matched by the specified DataFilter the specified values are applied to all of those ranges. Can be provided as a JSON string or as a list of DataFilterValueRange objects.

inputs.name: values
inputs.description: The data to be written. A two-dimensional array of values that will be written to the range. Values can be strings, numbers, or booleans. If the range is larger than the values array, the excess cells will not be changed. If the values array is larger than the range, the excess values will be ignored.

inputs.name: dataFilter
inputs.description: The data filter describing the criteria to select cells for update.

inputs.name: a1Range
inputs.description: The A1 notation of the range to update.

inputs.name: gridRange
inputs.description: Selects data within the range described by a GridRange. This field is optional. If specified, the dataFilter selects data within the specified grid range.

inputs.name: sheetId
inputs.description: The sheet this range is on.

inputs.name: endRowIndex
inputs.description: The end row (exclusive) of the range, or not set if unbounded.

inputs.name: startRowIndex
inputs.description: The start row (inclusive) of the range, or not set if unbounded.

inputs.name: endColumnIndex
inputs.description: The end column (exclusive) of the range, or not set if unbounded.

inputs.name: startColumnIndex
inputs.description: The start column (inclusive) of the range, or not set if unbounded.

inputs.name: developerMetadataLookup
inputs.description: Matches the data against the developer metadata that's associated with the dimensions. The developer metadata should be created with the location type set to either ROW or COLUMN and the visibility set to DOCUMENT.

inputs.name: metadataId
inputs.description: The ID of the developer metadata to match. This field is optional. If specified, the lookup matches only the developer metadata with the specified ID.

inputs.name: visibility
inputs.description: The visibility of the developer metadata to match. This field is optional. If specified, the lookup matches only the developer metadata with the specified visibility.

inputs.name: metadataKey
inputs.description: The key of the developer metadata to match. This field is optional. If specified, the lookup matches only the developer metadata with the specified key.

inputs.name: locationType
inputs.description: The type of location this object is looking for. Valid values are ROW, COLUMN, and SHEET.

inputs.name: metadataValue
inputs.description: The value of the developer metadata to match. This field is optional. If specified, the lookup matches only the developer metadata with the specified value.

inputs.name: metadataLocation
inputs.description: The location of the developer metadata to match. This field is optional. If specified, the lookup matches only the developer metadata in the specified location.

inputs.name: sheetId
inputs.description: The ID of the sheet the location is on.

inputs.name: spreadsheet
inputs.description: True if the metadata location is the spreadsheet itself.

inputs.name: locationType
inputs.description: The type of location this object represents. This field is read-only.

inputs.name: dimensionRange
inputs.description: A range along a single dimension on a sheet. All indexes are 0-based. Indexes are half open: the start index is inclusive and the end index is exclusive. Missing indexes indicate the range is unbounded on that side.

inputs.name: locationMatchingStrategy
inputs.description: Determines how this lookup matches the location. If this field is specified as EXACT, then the lookup requires an exact match of the specified locationType, metadataKey, and metadataValue. If this field is specified as INTERSECTING, then the lookup considers all metadata that intersects the specified locationType, and then filters that metadata by the specified key and value. If this field is unspecified, it is treated as EXACT.

inputs.name: majorDimension
inputs.description: The major dimension of the values. The default value is ROWS.

inputs.name: spreadsheetId
inputs.description: The ID of the spreadsheet to update.

inputs.name: valueInputOption
inputs.description: How the input data should be interpreted. RAW: Values are stored exactly as entered, without parsing. USER_ENTERED: Values are parsed as if typed by a user (numbers stay numbers, strings prefixed with '=' become formulas, etc.). INPUT_VALUE_OPTION_UNSPECIFIED: Default input value option is not specified.

inputs.name: includeValuesInResponse
inputs.description: Determines if the update response should include the values of the cells that were updated. By default, responses do not include the updated values.

inputs.name: responseValueRenderOption
inputs.description: Determines how values in the response should be rendered. The default render option is FORMATTED_VALUE.

inputs.name: responseDateTimeRenderOption
inputs.description: Determines how dates, times, and durations in the response should be rendered. This is ignored if responseValueRenderOption is FORMATTED_VALUE. The default dateTime render option is SERIAL_NUMBER.

requiredInputs.name: data

name: Bulk Insert Tasks (Deprecated)
description: DEPRECATED: Use BatchExecute instead. Creates multiple tasks in a Google Tasks list in a single operation using HTTP batching. Use when you need to create many tasks efficiently (reducing round-trips compared to individual insert calls).

inputs.name: tasks
inputs.description: Array of tasks to create. Each task requires at least a title.

inputs.name: due
inputs.description: Due date for the task in RFC3339 format (e.g., '2025-09-22T00:00:00Z'). Google Tasks only stores the date portion.

inputs.name: notes
inputs.description: Additional details or description for the task. Maximum 8192 characters.

inputs.name: title
inputs.description: Title/name of the task (REQUIRED). Maximum 1024 characters.

inputs.name: parent
inputs.description: Identifier of parent task to create this as a subtask.

inputs.name: status
inputs.description: Status of the task: 'needsAction' or 'completed'. Default: 'needsAction'

inputs.name: previous
inputs.description: Identifier of task after which this task should be placed.

inputs.name: completed
inputs.description: Completion timestamp in RFC3339 format. Only applicable when status='completed'.

inputs.name: batch_size
inputs.description: Maximum number of tasks per batch request. Default: 50. Tasks exceeding this will be split into multiple batch calls.

inputs.name: tasklist_id
inputs.description: Identifier for the Google Task list where tasks will be created. Use actual task list IDs, not '@default'.

requiredInputs.name: tasks
requiredInputs.description: Array of tasks to create. Each task requires at least a title.

requiredInputs.name: title

name: Get Single Calendar by ID
description: Retrieves metadata for a SINGLE specific calendar from the user's calendar list by its calendar ID. This action requires a calendarId parameter and returns details about that one calendar only. NOTE: This does NOT list all calendars. To list all calendars in the user's calendar list, use GOOGLECALENDAR_CALENDAR_LIST_LIST instead.

inputs.name: calendarId
inputs.description: Required. The calendar identifier for the single calendar to retrieve. Use 'primary' for the primary calendar of the authenticated user, or provide a specific calendar ID (e.g., an email address or group calendar ID). To find calendar IDs, first use GOOGLECALENDAR_CALENDAR_LIST_LIST to list all calendars.

name: Insert Calendar into List
description: Inserts an existing calendar into the user's calendar list, making it visible in the UI. Calendars (e.g., newly created ones) won't appear in the list or UI until explicitly inserted.

inputs.name: id
inputs.description: The identifier of the calendar to insert. Must be a valid calendar ID in email format (e.g., ‘user@example.com’ for a user’s calendar or ‘calendarid@group.calendar.google.com’ for a shared calendar). Note: The ‘primary’ keyword is not supported for this operation - use the actual email address of the primary calendar instead. The calendar must exist and you must have appropriate access permissions.

inputs.name: hidden
inputs.description: Whether the calendar has been hidden from the list. Accepts only boolean values: true or false. If not specified, the API defaults to false.

inputs.name: colorId
inputs.description: The color of the calendar. This is an ID referring to an entry in the calendarCore color palette.

inputs.name: selected
inputs.description: Whether the calendar is selected and visible in the calendar list. Accepts only boolean values: true or false. If not specified, the API defaults to false.

inputs.name: backgroundColor
inputs.description: The background color of the calendar in the Web UI. (Hexadecimal color code)

inputs.name: foregroundColor
inputs.description: The foreground color of the calendar in the Web UI. (Hexadecimal color code)

inputs.name: summaryOverride
inputs.description: The summary that the authenticated user has set for this calendar.

inputs.name: color_rgb_format
inputs.description: Whether to use the foregroundColor and backgroundColor fields to write the calendar colors (RGB). If this feature is used, the index-based colorId field will be set to the best matching option automatically. Optional. The default is False.

inputs.name: defaultReminders
inputs.description: The default reminders that the authenticated user has for this calendar.

inputs.name: method
inputs.description: The method used by this reminder (e.g., 'email', 'popup').

inputs.name: minutes
inputs.description: Number of minutes before the start of the event when the reminder should trigger.

inputs.name: notificationSettings
inputs.description: The notifications that the authenticated user is receiving for this calendar.

inputs.name: notifications
inputs.description: The list of notifications.

inputs.name: type
inputs.description: The type of notification (e.g., 'eventCreation', 'eventChange').

inputs.name: method
inputs.description: The method used by this notification (e.g., 'email').

name: Watch Calendar List
description: Watch for changes to CalendarList resources using push notifications. Use this to receive real-time updates when calendar list entries are modified.

inputs.name: id
inputs.description: A UUID or similar unique string that identifies this channel. Maximum 64 characters.

inputs.name: type
inputs.description: The type of delivery mechanism used for this channel. Must be "web_hook" or "webhook".

inputs.name: token
inputs.description: An arbitrary string delivered to the target address with each notification. Maximum 256 characters. Used for channel verification and message routing.

inputs.name: params
inputs.description: Additional parameters controlling delivery channel behavior.

inputs.name: ttl
inputs.description: Time-to-live in seconds for the notification channel. Default is 604800 seconds (7 days).

inputs.name: address
inputs.description: The HTTPS URL where notifications are delivered for this channel. Must have a valid SSL certificate.

inputs.name: expiration
inputs.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds indicating when the API should stop sending notifications.

name: Delete Calendar
description: Deletes a secondary calendar that you own or have delete permissions on. Deletion is permanent and irreversible — verify the correct calendar_id before calling. You cannot delete your primary calendar or calendars you only have read/write access to. Use calendarList.list to find calendars with owner accessRole. For primary calendars, use calendars.clear instead. Parallel calls may trigger userRateLimitExceeded; sequence bulk deletions.

inputs.name: calendar_id
inputs.description: Calendar identifier for a secondary calendar you own or have delete permissions on. Use calendarList.list to find deletable calendar IDs (look for accessRole "owner"). Primary calendars cannot be deleted; use the Clear Calendar action instead.

requiredInputs.name: calendar_id
requiredInputs.description: Calendar identifier for a secondary calendar you own or have delete permissions on. Use calendarList.list to find deletable calendar IDs (look for accessRole "owner"). Primary calendars cannot be deleted; use the Clear Calendar action instead.

name: Update Calendar
description: Full PUT-style update that overwrites all calendar metadata fields; unspecified optional fields are cleared. Use GOOGLECALENDAR_PATCH_CALENDAR to update only a subset of fields. Mutates the underlying calendar resource (title, description, timeZone, etc.); use GOOGLECALENDAR_CALENDAR_LIST_UPDATE to change per-user display properties like color.

inputs.name: summary
inputs.description: Title of the calendar. Must be a non-empty string; passing an empty string clears the calendar title.

inputs.name: location
inputs.description: Geographic location of the calendar as free-form text. Optional.

name: Check Compatibility
description: Tool to list dimensions and metrics compatible with a GA4 report request. Use when you need to validate compatibility of chosen dimensions or metrics before running a report.

inputs.name: metrics
inputs.description: Optional. List of metrics to check compatibility for. Maximum of 10 metrics allowed per request.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The GA4 API name of the metric. MUST use exact API names from the official documentation at https://developers.google.com/analytics/devguides/reporting/data/v1/api-schema. Common valid metric names: Users: 'activeUsers', 'totalUsers', 'newUsers'. Sessions: 'sessions', 'engagedSessions', 'bounceRate', 'engagementRate', 'averageSessionDuration'. Events: 'eventCount', 'eventCountPerUser', 'keyEvents', 'eventValue'. Page/Screen: 'screenPageViews', 'screenPageViewsPerSession'. E-commerce: 'purchaseRevenue', 'totalRevenue', 'transactions', 'itemRevenue'. Conversions: 'conversions', 'userConversionRate' (NOTE: 'conversionRate' is NOT valid - use 'sessionConversionRate' or 'userConversionRate' instead). Custom metrics use format 'customEvent:parameter_name'.

inputs.name: property
inputs.description: Required. GA4 property resource name. Format: properties/{property_id}.

inputs.name: dimensions
inputs.description: Optional. List of dimensions to check compatibility for.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The GA4 API name of the dimension. MUST use exact API names from the official documentation at https://developers.google.com/analytics/devguides/reporting/data/v1/api-schema. Common valid dimension names include: User/Session: 'newVsReturning', 'firstSessionDate'. Time: 'date', 'dateHour', 'day', 'dayOfWeek', 'hour', 'month', 'week', 'year'. Geography: 'country', 'countryId', 'region', 'city', 'continent'. Technology: 'browser', 'operatingSystem', 'deviceCategory', 'platform', 'language', 'screenResolution'. Page/Content: 'pagePath', 'pageTitle', 'pageLocation', 'landingPage', 'hostName'. Traffic Source: 'source', 'medium', 'campaignName', 'defaultChannelGroup', 'manualSource', 'manualMedium' (NOTE: 'trafficSource' is NOT valid - use 'source' or 'medium' instead). Events: 'eventName', 'isKeyEvent'. E-commerce: 'itemId', 'itemName', 'itemBrand', 'itemCategory', 'currencyCode'. Custom dimensions use format 'customUser:parameter_name' or 'customEvent:parameter_name'.

inputs.name: metricFilter
inputs.description: Optional. A FilterExpression for metrics; must follow GA4 FilterExpression JSON schema.

inputs.name: dimensionFilter
inputs.description: Optional. A FilterExpression for dimensions; must follow GA4 FilterExpression JSON schema.

inputs.name: compatibilityFilter
inputs.description:
Compatibility status for dimensions or metrics per Google Analytics Data API v1beta.
Valid values: COMPATIBILITY_UNSPECIFIED, COMPATIBLE, INCOMPATIBLE.
Note: Values like 'REPORT_COMPATIBLE' are NOT valid - use 'COMPATIBLE' instead.

requiredInputs.name: property

optionalInputs.name: metrics

Events: 'eventCount', 'eventCountPerUser', 'keyEvents', 'eventValue'. Page/Screen: 'screenPageViews', 'screenPageViewsPerSession'. E-commerce: 'purchaseRevenue', 'totalRevenue', 'transactions', 'itemRevenue'. Conversions: 'conversions', 'userConversionRate' (NOTE: 'conversionRate' is NOT valid - use 'sessionConversionRate' or 'userConversionRate' instead). Custom metrics use format 'customEvent:parameter_name'.

optionalInputs.name: dimensions

name: Compute Route Matrix
description: Calculates travel distance and duration matrix between multiple origins and destinations using the modern Routes API; supports OAuth2 authentication and various travel modes. Matrix is capped at 625 elements (e.g., 25×25); chunk larger sets to avoid RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED errors. Response elements may be returned out of input order — always use originIndex and destinationIndex to map results. Only use elements where condition='ROUTE_EXISTS'; the matrix may be incomplete.

inputs.name: units
inputs.description: Unit system (e.g., 'METRIC' for kilometers, 'IMPERIAL' for miles) for displaying distances. Note: distanceMeters in responses is always in meters regardless of this setting; units affects only human-readable text output.

inputs.name: origins
inputs.description: List of origin locations. Each can be specified as an address or latitude/longitude coordinates.

inputs.name: address
inputs.description: Address as a string. Provide either address or latitude/longitude.

inputs.name: latitude
inputs.description: Latitude in decimal degrees. Must be used with longitude.

inputs.name: longitude
inputs.description: Longitude in decimal degrees. Must be used with latitude.

inputs.name: fieldMask
inputs.description: Comma-separated list of response fields to include (e.g., 'originIndex,destinationIndex,duration,distanceMeters'). Use '*' for all fields.

inputs.name: travelMode
inputs.description: Mode of transportation for the route matrix calculation.

inputs.name: destinations
inputs.description: List of destination locations. Each can be specified as an address or latitude/longitude coordinates.

inputs.name: address
inputs.description: Address as a string. Provide either address or latitude/longitude.

inputs.name: latitude
inputs.description: Latitude in decimal degrees. Must be used with longitude.

inputs.name: longitude
inputs.description: Longitude in decimal degrees. Must be used with latitude.

inputs.name: languageCode
inputs.description: BCP-47 language code (e.g., 'en-US', 'es') for textual information.

inputs.name: routingPreference
inputs.description: Specifies factors to consider when calculating the route.

name: Copy Google Document
description: Tool to create a copy of an existing Google Document. Use this to duplicate a document, for example, when using an existing document as a template. The copied document will have a default title (e.g., 'Copy of [original title]') if no new title is provided, and will be placed in the user's root Google Drive folder.

name: Copy file (Deprecated)
description: DEPRECATED: Use GOOGLEDRIVE_COPY_FILE_ADVANCED instead. Duplicates an existing file (not folders) in Google Drive by `file_id`; copy lands in same folder as original — use GOOGLEDRIVE_MOVE_FILE afterward for precise placement. Copy receives a new `file_id`; update stored references accordingly. For shared drives, requires organizer/manager rights.

inputs.name: file_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier for the file on Google Drive that you want to copy. This ID can be retrieved from the file's shareable link or via other Google Drive API calls. Pass only the raw ID, not a full URL. Name-based searches may return multiple files — confirm the correct `file_id` before calling.

inputs.name: new_title
inputs.description: The title to assign to the new copy of the file. If not provided, the copied file will have the same title as the original, prefixed with 'Copy of '.
