name: List Files and Folders (Deprecated)
description: DEPRECATED: Use GOOGLEDRIVE_FIND_FILE instead. Tool to list a user's files and folders in Google Drive. Use this to search or browse for files and folders based on various criteria.

inputs.name: q
inputs.description: A query string for filtering the file results. Supports operators 'and', 'or', 'not'. VALID query terms: 'name' (contains, =, !=), 'fullText' (contains), 'mimeType' (contains, =, !=), 'modifiedTime' (<=, <, =, !=, >, >=), 'viewedByMeTime' (<=, <, =, !=, >, >=), 'trashed' (=, !=), 'starred' (=, !=), 'parents' (in), 'owners' (in), 'writers' (in), 'readers' (in), 'sharedWithMe' (=, !=), 'createdTime' (<=, <, =, !=, >, >=), 'properties' (has), 'appProperties' (has), 'visibility' (=, !=), 'shortcutDetails.targetId' (=, !=). IMPORTANT: 'id' is NOT a valid query term - you cannot search by file ID using this parameter. To get a specific file by ID, use the 'Get File Metadata' action instead. Example: "name contains 'important' and mimeType = 'application/vnd.google-apps.folder'".

inputs.name: fields
inputs.description: Selector specifying which file fields to include in the response. Provide a comma-separated list of file field names (e.g., 'id,name,mimeType,webViewLink'). The action will automatically format this into the proper API format 'files(field1,field2,...)'. Common file fields include: id, name, description, mimeType, webViewLink, webContentLink, size, createdTime, modifiedTime, parents, owners, permissions. To also include the pagination token, add 'nextPageToken' to the list. NOTE: Google Drive API v2 field names are automatically converted to v3 equivalents (e.g., alternateLink→webViewLink, downloadUrl→webContentLink, title→name, createdDate→createdTime, modifiedDate→modifiedTime).

inputs.name: spaces
inputs.description: A comma-separated list of spaces to query within the corpora. Supported values are 'drive' and 'appDataFolder'. 'drive' represents files in My Drive and shared drives, while 'appDataFolder' represents the application's private data folder.

inputs.name: corpora
inputs.description: Specifies the bodies of items (files/documents) to which the query applies. Supported values are 'user', 'domain', 'drive', and 'allDrives'. It's generally more efficient to use 'user' or 'drive' instead of 'allDrives'. Defaults to 'user'.

inputs.name: driveId
inputs.description: The ID of the shared drive to search. This is used when `corpora` is set to 'drive'.

inputs.name: orderBy
inputs.description: A comma-separated list of sort keys. Valid keys are: 'createdTime', 'folder', 'modifiedByMeTime', 'modifiedTime', 'name', 'name_natural', 'quotaBytesUsed', 'recency', 'sharedWithMeTime', 'starred', 'viewedByMeTime'. IMPORTANT: Use 'quotaBytesUsed' to sort by file size (do NOT use 'size' - it is not a valid key). Each key sorts in ascending order by default, but can be reversed with the 'desc' modifier (e.g., 'modifiedTime desc').

inputs.name: folderId
inputs.description: ID of a specific folder to list files from. This is a convenience parameter that automatically adds "'folder_id' in parents" to the query. Cannot be used together with a custom 'q' parameter.

inputs.name: pageSize
inputs.description: The maximum number of files to return per page. The value must be between 1 and 1000, inclusive. Defaults to 100.

inputs.name: pageToken
inputs.description: The token for continuing a previous list request on the next page. This MUST be set to the value of 'nextPageToken' from the previous response. Do not manually construct or modify pageToken values as they are opaque tokens generated by the API. If the token is rejected, pagination should be restarted from the first page.

inputs.name: includeLabels
inputs.description: A comma-separated list of label IDs to include in the `labelInfo` part of the response for each file.

inputs.name: supportsAllDrives
inputs.description: Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives. Defaults to false. If true, then `includeItemsFromAllDrives` can be used to extend the search to all drives.

inputs.name: includeItemsFromAllDrives
inputs.description: Whether to include items from both My Drive and shared drives. This is relevant when `corpora` is 'user' or 'domain'. Defaults to false.

inputs.name: includePermissionsForView
inputs.description: Include additional permissions for a specific view. The only valid value is 'published', which includes permissions for files with published content. Omit this parameter if you don't need published view permissions.

optionalInputs.name: q
optionalInputs.description: A query string for filtering the file results. Supports operators 'and', 'or', 'not'. VALID query terms: 'name' (contains, =, !=), 'fullText' (contains), 'mimeType' (contains, =, !=), 'modifiedTime' (<=, <, =, !=, >, >=), 'viewedByMeTime' (<=, <, =, !=, >, >=), 'trashed' (=, !=), 'starred' (=, !=), 'parents' (in), 'owners' (in), 'writers' (in), 'readers' (in), 'sharedWithMe' (=, !=), 'createdTime' (<=, <, =, !=, >, >=), 'properties' (has), 'appProperties' (has), 'visibility' (=, !=), 'shortcutDetails.targetId' (=, !=). name: List Gmail history
description: Tool to list Gmail mailbox change history since a known startHistoryId. Use for incremental mailbox syncs. Persist the latest historyId as a checkpoint across sessions; without it, incremental sync is unreliable. An empty history list in the response is valid and means no new changes occurred.

inputs.name: user_id
inputs.description: The user's email address. Use 'me' to specify the authenticated user.

inputs.name: label_id
inputs.description: Only return history records involving messages with this label ID.

inputs.name: page_token
inputs.description: Token to retrieve a specific page of results. If the response includes nextPageToken, loop requests using this parameter until no nextPageToken is returned; failing to paginate will silently miss changes.

inputs.name: max_results
inputs.description: Maximum number of history records to return. Default is 100; max is 500.

inputs.name: history_types
inputs.description: Filter by specific history types. Allowed values: messageAdded, messageDeleted, labelAdded, labelRemoved.

inputs.name: start_history_id
inputs.description: Required. Returns history records after this ID. If the ID is invalid or too old, the API returns 404. Perform a full sync in that case. Should be a numeric string. On 404 (historyIdTooOld) or 400 (invalidArgument), recover by fetching a fresh historyId via GMAIL_GET_PROFILE, then perform a one-time full sync via GMAIL_FETCH_EMAILS before resuming incremental calls.

requiredInputs.name: start_history_id
requiredInputs.description: Required. Returns history records after this ID. If the ID is invalid or too old, the API returns 404. Perform a full sync in that case. Should be a numeric string. On 404 (historyIdTooOld) or 400 (invalidArgument), recover by fetching a fresh historyId via GMAIL_GET_PROFILE, then perform a one-time full sync via GMAIL_FETCH_EMAILS before resuming incremental calls.

optionalInputs.name: user_id
optionalInputs.description: The user's email address. Use 'me' to specify the authenticated user.

optionalInputs.name: label_id
optionalInputs.description: Only return history records involving messages with this label ID.

optionalInputs.name: history_types
optionalInputs.description: Filter by specific history types. Allowed values: messageAdded, messageDeleted, labelAdded, labelRemoved.

Do not infer key-event status from report data (e.g., eventCount); use this tool to confirm.

name: List Gmail labels
description: Retrieves all system and user-created labels for a Gmail account in a single unpaginated response. Primary use: obtain internal label IDs (e.g., 'Label_123') required by other Gmail tools — display names cannot be used as label identifiers and cause silent failures or errors. System labels (INBOX, UNREAD, SPAM, TRASH, etc.) are case-sensitive and must be used exactly as returned; INBOX, SPAM, and TRASH are read-only and cannot be added/removed via label modification tools. The Gmail search 'label:' operator accepts display names, but label_ids parameters in tools like GMAIL_FETCH_EMAILS require internal IDs from this tool — mixing conventions yields zero results silently. Do not hardcode label IDs across sessions; refresh via this tool on conflict errors.

inputs.name: user_id
inputs.description: Identifies the Gmail account (owner's email or 'me' for authenticated user) for which labels will be listed.

inputs.name: include_details
inputs.description: If true, fetches detailed info for each label including message/thread counts (messagesTotal, messagesUnread, threadsTotal, threadsUnread). This requires additional API calls and may be slower for accounts with many labels. If false (default), returns basic label info (id, name, type) which is faster. Counts are eventually consistent and may lag real-time mailbox state by a few seconds.

optionalInputs.name: user_id
optionalInputs.description: Identifies the Gmail account (owner's email or 'me' for authenticated user) for which labels will be listed.

optionalInputs.name: include_details
optionalInputs.description: If true, fetches detailed info for each label including message/thread counts (messagesTotal, messagesUnread, threadsTotal, threadsUnread). This requires additional API calls and may be slower for accounts with many labels. If false (default), returns basic label info (id, name, type) which is faster. Counts are eventually consistent and may lag real-time mailbox state by a few seconds.

name: List Media Items (App-Created Only)
description: Lists media items created by this application from Google Photos. DEPRECATION NOTICE: As of March 31, 2025, the Google Photos Library API ONLY returns media items that were uploaded/created by your application. This action CANNOT access the user's full photo library. Use cases this action SUPPORTS: - Listing photos/videos your app previously uploaded to the user's library - Managing app-created content in Google Photos Use cases this action DOES NOT SUPPORT: - Accessing photos taken by the user's camera - Viewing photos from other apps or web uploads - Listing the user's entire photo library For accessing a user's full library, use the Google Photos Picker API instead: https://developers.google.com/photos/picker

name: List Participant Sessions
description: Lists all participant sessions for a specific participant in a Google Meet conference. A participant session represents each unique join or leave session when a user joins a conference from a device. If a user joins multiple times from the same device, each join creates a new session. Returns session details including start time and end time for each session.

inputs.name: filter
inputs.description: Optional. User-specified filtering condition in EBNF format. Filterable fields include `start_time` and `end_time`. Time values must use RFC3339 UTC format (e.g., `start_time >= '2024-01-01T00:00:00Z'`). Unsupported field names or malformed expressions may silently narrow or drop results rather than raising errors. Reversed bounds or incorrect timezone conversion will produce empty results.

inputs.name: page_size
inputs.description: Optional. Maximum number of participant sessions to return. The service might return fewer than this value. If unspecified, at most 100 participant sessions are returned. The maximum value is 250; values above 250 are coerced to 250.

inputs.name: page_token
inputs.description: Optional. Page token returned from a previous list call, used to retrieve the next page of results. Iterate using successive `page_token` values until the response omits `page_token` to avoid silently missing sessions.

inputs.name: participant_id
inputs.description: Required. The unique identifier of the participant within the conference. Example: 'xyz-456'.

inputs.name: conference_record_id
inputs.description: Required. The unique identifier of the conference record. Example: 'abc-123-def'.

requiredInputs.name: participant_id
requiredInputs.description: Required. The unique identifier of the participant within the conference. Example: 'xyz-456'.

name: List Shared Drives
description: Tool to list the user's shared drives. Use when you need to get a list of all shared drives accessible to the authenticated user. Results may differ from the web UI due to admin policies; listing a drive does not guarantee access to its contents. Paginated calls may trigger 403 rateLimitExceeded or 429 tooManyRequests; apply exponential backoff when iterating many pages.

inputs.name: q
inputs.description: Query string for searching shared drives using Google Drive query syntax (e.g., "name contains 'ProjectX'" or "createdTime > '2023-01-01T00:00:00'"). Query format: query_term operator values. Common query terms: name, createdTime, memberCount, organizerCount, hidden. Common operators: contains, =, >, <, >=, !=. String values must be enclosed in single quotes. Special characters (apostrophes, backslashes) must be escaped. Multiple terms can be combined with 'and'/'or' operators and parentheses for grouping.

inputs.name: pageSize
inputs.description: Maximum number of shared drives to return per page. Maximum allowed value is 1000. Paginate by passing the returned nextPageToken back as pageToken until no nextPageToken is returned to avoid silently missing drives.

inputs.name: pageToken
inputs.description: Page token for shared drives.

inputs.name: useDomainAdminAccess
inputs.description: Issue the request as a domain administrator. If set to true, then all shared drives of the domain in which the requester is an administrator are returned.

optionalInputs.name: q
optionalInputs.description: Query string for searching shared drives using Google Drive query syntax (e.g., "name contains 'ProjectX'" or "createdTime > '2023-01-01T00:00:00'"). String values must be enclosed in single quotes. Special characters (apostrophes, backslashes) must be escaped. Multiple terms can be combined with 'and'/'or' operators and parentheses for grouping.

optionalInputs.name: pageSize

name: List Tables in Spreadsheet
description: DEPRECATED: Use GOOGLESHEETS_GET_SHEET_NAMES for tab discovery and GOOGLESHEETS_GET_SPREADSHEET_INFO for full sheet metadata. This action is used to list all tables in a Google Spreadsheet, call this action to get the list of tables in a spreadsheet. Discover all tables in a Google Spreadsheet by analyzing sheet structure and detecting data patterns. Uses heuristic analysis to find header rows, data boundaries, and table structures.

inputs.description: Minimum number of data rows to consider a valid table

inputs.name: min_columns
inputs.description: Minimum number of columns to consider a valid table

inputs.name: min_confidence
inputs.description: Minimum confidence score (0.0-1.0) to consider a valid table

inputs.name: spreadsheet_id
inputs.description: The actual Google Spreadsheet ID (not a placeholder or spreadsheet name). Find it in the spreadsheet URL: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/{SPREADSHEET_ID}/edit. It is the alphanumeric string between '/d/' and '/edit' (e.g., '1BxiMVs0XRA5nFMdKvBdBZjgmUUqptlbs74OgvE2upms'). IMPORTANT: Do NOT pass the spreadsheet name - only pass the alphanumeric ID from the URL. Do NOT pass template placeholders like '{{spreadsheet_id}}', '<spreadsheet_id>', or 'your-spreadsheet-id-here'.

name: List Tasks
description: Retrieves tasks from a Google Tasks list; all date/time strings must be RFC3339 UTC, and `showCompleted` must be true if `completedMin` or `completedMax` are specified. Response key for tasks is `tasks` (not `items`). No full-text search; filter client-side by title/notes. Results ordered by position, not by date.

inputs.name: dueMax
inputs.description:
Exclude tasks due after this date. 
Google Tasks API requires dates in RFC3339 format (e.g., 2025-09-22T13:48:27Z).
We automatically convert various human-readable formats to RFC3339:

Accepted formats:
• 2025-09-28T23:59:00Z → RFC3339/ISO-8601 format (preferred) - September 28, 2025 at 11:59 PM UTC
• 28 Sep 2025 → Simple date format - Will be converted to midnight UTC on that date
• 11:59 PM, 22 Sep 2025 → Time with date - Assumes UTC timezone if not specified
• 1:00 PM, 21 Sep 2025 → 12-hour format with date - Converted to 13:00 UTC
• UTC-5:30, 6:50 PM → With timezone offset - Converted to UTC automatically
• UTC+1, 11:59 PM, 31 Dec → Timezone with date - Next occurrence of Dec 31
• 2025-09-21T15:30:00+02:00 → RFC3339 with timezone offset

Note: If no timezone is specified, UTC is assumed. Dates without times default to midnight UTC.

• UTC+1, 11:59 PM, 31 Dec → Timezone with date - Next occurrence of Dec 31
• 2025-09-21T15:30:00+02:00 → RFC3339 with timezone offset

Note: If no timezone is specified, UTC is assumed. Dates without times default to midnight UTC.

name: List threads
description: Retrieves a list of email threads from a Gmail account, identified by `user_id` (email address or 'me'), supporting filtering and pagination. Spam and trash are excluded by default unless explicitly targeted via `label:spam` or `label:trash` in the query.

inputs.name: query
inputs.description: Filter for threads, using Gmail search query syntax (e.g., 'from:user@example.com is:unread'). Supported operators include `from:`, `to:`, `subject:`, `label:`, `is:unread`, `has:attachment`, `after:`, `before:`. Dates must use `YYYY/MM/DD` format; date operators are UTC-based. Exact subject phrases require quotes (e.g., `subject:'meeting notes'`).

inputs.name: user_id
inputs.description: The user's email address or 'me' to specify the authenticated Gmail account.

inputs.name: verbose
inputs.description: If false, returns threads with basic fields (id, snippet, historyId). If true, returns threads with complete message details including headers, body, attachments, and metadata for each message in the thread. Combining `verbose=true` with large `max_results` produces very large responses; keep `max_results` modest when verbose is enabled.

inputs.name: page_token
inputs.description: Token from a previous response to retrieve a specific page of results; omit for the first page.

inputs.name: max_results
inputs.description: Maximum number of threads to return. Hard cap is ~500 per call. For full mailbox coverage, loop using `nextPageToken` via `page_token` until absent.

optionalInputs.name: query
optionalInputs.description: Filter for threads, using Gmail search query syntax (e.g., 'from:user@example.com is:unread'). Supported operators include `from:`, `to:`, `subject:`, `label:`, `is:unread`, `has:attachment`, `after:`, `before:`. Dates must use `YYYY/MM/DD` format; date operators are UTC-based. Exact subject phrases require quotes (e.g., `subject:'meeting notes'`).

optionalInputs.name: user_id
optionalInputs.description: The user's email address or 'me' to specify the authenticated Gmail account.

optionalInputs.name: verbose
optionalInputs.description: If false, returns threads with basic fields (id, snippet, historyId). If true, returns threads with complete message details including headers, body, attachments, and metadata for each message in the thread. Combining `verbose=true` with large `max_results` produces very large responses; keep `max_results` modest when verbose is enabled.

optionalInputs.name: page_token
optionalInputs.description: Token from a previous response to retrieve a specific page of results; omit for the first page.

optionalInputs.name: max_results
optionalInputs.description: Maximum number of threads to return. Hard cap is ~500 per call. For full mailbox coverage, loop using `nextPageToken` via `page_token` until absent.

name: List Transcript Entries
description: Tool to list structured transcript entries (speaker/time/text segments) for a specific Google Meet transcript. Use when you need to access the detailed content of a transcript, including individual spoken segments with timestamps and speaker information. Note: The transcript entries returned by the API might not match the transcription in Google Docs due to interleaved speakers or post-generation modifications.

inputs.name: page_size
inputs.description: Maximum number of transcript entries to return. The service might return fewer than this value. If unspecified, at most 10 entries are returned. The maximum value is 100; values above 100 are coerced to 100.

inputs.description: Page token returned from a previous list call, used to retrieve the next page of results.

inputs.description: The unique identifier of the transcript within the conference record.

name: Look up spreadsheet row
description: Finds the first row in a Google Spreadsheet where a cell's entire content exactly matches the query string, searching within a specified A1 notation range or the first sheet by default.

inputs.name: query
inputs.description: Exact text value to find; matches the entire content of a cell in a row.

inputs.name: range
inputs.description: A1 notation range to search within. Supports cell ranges (e.g., 'Sheet1!A1:D5'), column-only ranges (e.g., 'Sheet1!A:Z'), and row-only ranges (e.g., 'Sheet1!1:1'). Defaults to the first sheet if omitted. IMPORTANT: Sheet names with spaces must be single-quoted (e.g., "'My Sheet'!A1:Z"). Bare sheet names without ranges (e.g., 'Sheet1') are not supported - always specify a range.

inputs.name: case_sensitive
inputs.description: If `True`, the query string search is case-sensitive.

inputs.name: spreadsheet_id
inputs.description: Identifier of the Google Spreadsheet to search.

inputs.name: value_render_option
inputs.description: How cell values are rendered in the returned row data. unformatted: raw values without display formatting — dates appear as serial numbers, e.g. 46009.47 (default, keeps consistency with UPSERT). formatted: display-formatted values — dates appear as strings, e.g. '2025-12-18'. formula: raw formulas instead of computed values.

inputs.name: normalize_whitespace
inputs.description: If `True`, strips leading and trailing whitespace from cell values before matching. This helps match cells like ' TOTAL ' or 'TOTAL ' when searching for 'TOTAL'.

inputs.name: date_time_render_option
inputs.description: How dates and times are represented. FORMATTED_STRING: human-readable strings (e.g. '2025-12-18 11:17'). SERIAL_NUMBER: Excel-style serial numbers. Works with all value_render_option settings.

requiredInputs.name: query
requiredInputs.description: Exact text value to find; matches the entire content of a cell in a row.

requiredInputs.name: spreadsheet_id
requiredInputs.description: Identifier of the Google Spreadsheet to search.

optionalInputs.name: range
optionalInputs.description: A1 notation range to search within. Supports cell ranges (e.g., 'Sheet1!A1:D5'), column-only ranges (e.g., 'Sheet1!A:Z'), and row-only ranges (e.g., 'Sheet1!1:1'). Defaults to the first sheet if omitted. IMPORTANT: Sheet names with spaces must be single-quoted (e.g., "'My Sheet'!A1:Z"). Bare sheet names without ranges (e.g., 'Sheet1') are not supported - always specify a range.

optionalInputs.name: case_sensitive
optionalInputs.description: If `True`, the query string search is case-sensitive.

optionalInputs.name: value_render_option
optionalInputs.description: How cell values are rendered in the returned row data. unformatted: raw values without display formatting — dates appear as serial numbers, e.g. 46009.47 (default, keeps consistency with UPSERT). formatted: display-formatted values — dates appear as strings, e.g. '2025-12-18'. formula: raw formulas instead of computed values.

name: Embed Google Map
description: Tool to generate an embeddable Google Map URL and HTML iframe code. Use when you need to display a map (place, view, directions, street view, search) on a webpage without JavaScript. Note: This API only works with API keys (no OAuth2 support). It generates embed URLs and does not make direct API calls. Generated embed URLs are publicly accessible; avoid passing sensitive or internal location queries.

inputs.name: mode
inputs.description: The mode of the embedded map.

inputs.name: api_key
inputs.description: Google Maps API key. Required if not provided via connection metadata. The Maps Embed API only supports API key authentication, not OAuth.

inputs.name: view_params
inputs.description: Parameters for 'view' mode.

inputs.name: zoom
inputs.description: Sets the initial zoom level of the map. Values range from 0 (the whole world) to 21 (individual buildings).

inputs.name: center
inputs.description: Defines the center of the map view. Accepts comma-separated latitude and longitude value (e.g., '37.4218,-122.0840'). This is REQUIRED for view mode.

inputs.name: region
inputs.description: Defines appropriate borders and labels based on geopolitical sensitivities (e.g., 'US', 'GB'). Accepts a two-character ccTLD code.

inputs.name: maptype
inputs.description: Defines the type of map tiles to load.

inputs.name: language
inputs.description: Defines the language for UI elements and map labels (e.g., 'en', 'es', 'fr').

inputs.name: place_params
inputs.description: Parameters for 'place' mode.

inputs.name: q
inputs.description: Defines the map marker location. Accepts a URL-escaped place name, address, plus code, or Place ID (prefixed with 'place_id:').

inputs.name: zoom
inputs.description: Sets the initial zoom level of the map. Values range from 0 (the whole world) to 21 (individual buildings).

inputs.name: center
inputs.description: Defines the center of the map view. Accepts comma-separated latitude and longitude value (e.g., '37.4218,-122.0840').

inputs.name: region
inputs.description: Defines appropriate borders and labels based on geopolitical sensitivities (e.g., 'US', 'GB'). Accepts a two-character ccTLD code.

inputs.name: maptype
inputs.description: Defines the type of map tiles to load.

inputs.name: language
inputs.description: Defines the language for UI elements and map labels (e.g., 'en', 'es', 'fr').

inputs.name: search_params
inputs.description: Parameters for 'search' mode.

inputs.name: q
inputs.description: Defines the search term. Can include a geographic restriction (e.g., 'record+stores+in+Seattle').

inputs.name: zoom
inputs.description: Sets the initial zoom level of the map. Values range from 0 (the whole world) to 21 (individual buildings).

inputs.name: center
inputs.description: Defines the center of the map view. Accepts comma-separated latitude and longitude value (e.g., '37.4218,-122.0840').

inputs.name: region
inputs.description: Defines appropriate borders and labels based on geopolitical sensitivities (e.g., 'US', 'GB'). Accepts a two-character ccTLD code.

inputs.name: maptype
inputs.description: Defines the type of map tiles to load.

inputs.name: language
inputs.description: Defines the language for UI elements and map labels (e.g., 'en', 'es', 'fr').

inputs.name: directions_params
inputs.description: Parameters for 'directions' mode.

inputs.name: mode
inputs.description: Defines the method of travel.

inputs.name: zoom
inputs.description: Sets the initial zoom level of the map. Values range from 0 (the whole world) to 21 (individual buildings).

inputs.name: avoid
inputs.description: Specifies features to avoid in directions (e.g., 'tolls', 'highways', 'ferries'). Separate multiple values with the pipe character (|). E.g. 'tolls|highways'.

inputs.name: units
inputs.description: Specifies measurement units for distances.

inputs.name: center
inputs.description: Defines the center of the map view. Accepts comma-separated latitude and longitude value (e.g., '37.4218,-122.0840').

inputs.name: origin
inputs.description: Defines the starting point for directions. Accepts URL-escaped place name, address, plus code, lat/lng coordinates, or Place ID (prefixed with 'place_id:').

inputs.name: region
inputs.description: Defines appropriate borders and labels based on geopolitical sensitivities (e.g., 'US', 'GB'). Accepts a two-character ccTLD code.

inputs.name: maptype
inputs.description: Defines the type of map tiles to load.

inputs.name: language
inputs.description: Defines the language for UI elements and map labels (e.g., 'en', 'es', 'fr').

inputs.name: waypoints
inputs.description: Specifies one or more intermediary places to route directions. Separate multiple waypoints with the pipe character (|). E.g., 'Berlin,Germany|Paris,France'. Up to 20 waypoints.

inputs.name: destination
inputs.description: Defines the endpoint of the directions. Accepts URL-escaped place name, address, plus code, lat/lng coordinates, or Place ID (prefixed with 'place_id:').

inputs.name: streetview_params
inputs.description: Parameters for 'streetview' mode.

inputs.name: fov
inputs.description: Horizontal field of view in degrees (10-100). Smaller numbers mean higher zoom.

inputs.name: pano
inputs.description: A specific panorama ID. Required if 'location' is not provided. 'location' can be a fallback.

inputs.name: zoom
inputs.description: Sets the initial zoom level of the map. Values range from 0 (the whole world) to 21 (individual buildings).

inputs.name: pitch
inputs.description: Angle, up or down, of the camera in degrees (-90 to 90). Positive is up.

inputs.name: center
inputs.description: Defines the center of the map view. Accepts comma-separated latitude and longitude value (e.g., '37.4218,-122.0840').

inputs.name: radius
inputs.description: Radius in meters to search for a panorama, centered on lat/lng. Default 50.

inputs.name: region

inputs.name: source
inputs.description: Limits Street View searches to selected sources. 'default' or 'outdoor'.

inputs.name: heading
inputs.description: Compass heading of the camera in degrees clockwise from North (-180 to 360).

inputs.name: maptype
inputs.description: Defines the type of map tiles to load.

inputs.name: language
inputs.description: Defines the language for UI elements and map labels (e.g., 'en', 'es', 'fr').

inputs.name: location
inputs.description: Latitude and longitude as comma-separated values (e.g., '46.414382,10.013988'). Displays panorama closest to this location. Required if 'pano' is not provided.

name: Modify File Labels
description: Modifies the set of labels applied to a file. Returns a list of the labels that were added or modified. Use when you need to programmatically change labels on a Google Drive file, such as adding, updating, or removing them.

inputs.name: kind
inputs.description: This is always drive#modifyLabelsRequest.

inputs.name: file_id
inputs.description: The ID of the file.

inputs.name: label_modifications
inputs.description: The list of modifications to apply to the labels on the file.

inputs.name: kind
inputs.description: This is always drive#labelModification.

inputs.name: labelId
inputs.description: The internal label ID (NOT the label's display name). Must be a bare alphanumeric ID without any prefix (do NOT include 'labels/'). Obtain valid IDs using files.listLabels or the Drive Labels API.

inputs.name: removeLabel
inputs.description: If true, the label will be removed from the file.

inputs.name: fieldModifications
inputs.description: The list of modifications to this label's fields.

inputs.name: kind
inputs.description: This is always drive#labelFieldModification.

inputs.name: fieldId
inputs.description: The internal field ID from the label schema (NOT the field's display name). Must be a bare alphanumeric ID. Obtain valid IDs using files.listLabels or the Drive Labels API.

inputs.name: unsetValues
inputs.description: Unsets the values for this field.

inputs.name: setDateValues
inputs.description: Replaces the value of a `date` field with these new values. The string must be in the RFC 3339 full-date format: YYYY-MM-DD.

inputs.name: setTextValues
inputs.description: Sets the value of a `text` field.

inputs.name: setUserValues
inputs.description: Replaces a `user` field with these new values. The values must be valid email addresses.

inputs.name: setIntegerValues
inputs.description: Replaces the value of an `integer` field with these new values.

inputs.name: setSelectionValues
inputs.description: Replaces a `selection` field with these new values.

requiredInputs.name: file_id
requiredInputs.description: The ID of the file.

requiredInputs.name: label_modifications
requiredInputs.description: The list of modifications to apply to the labels on the file.

requiredInputs.name: labelId
requiredInputs.description: The internal label ID (NOT the label's display name). Must be a bare alphanumeric ID without any prefix (do NOT include 'labels/'). Obtain valid IDs using files.listLabels or the Drive Labels API.

optionalInputs.name: kind
optionalInputs.description: This is always drive#modifyLabelsRequest.

optionalInputs.name: kind
optionalInputs.description: This is always drive#labelModification.

optionalInputs.description: If true, the label will be removed from the file.

name: Modify thread labels
description: Adds or removes specified existing label IDs from a Gmail thread, affecting all its messages; ensure the thread ID is valid. To modify a single message only, use a message-level tool instead.

inputs.name: user_id
inputs.description: User's email address or 'me' for the authenticated user.

inputs.name: thread_id
inputs.description: Immutable ID of the thread to modify.

inputs.name: add_label_ids
inputs.description: List of label IDs to add to the thread. Must be valid label IDs that exist in the user's account. System labels use uppercase names (e.g., 'INBOX', 'STARRED', 'IMPORTANT', 'UNREAD', 'SPAM', 'TRASH', 'SENT', 'DRAFT', 'CATEGORY_PERSONAL', 'CATEGORY_SOCIAL', 'CATEGORY_PROMOTIONS', 'CATEGORY_UPDATES', 'CATEGORY_FORUMS'). Custom labels use the format 'Label_N' (e.g., 'Label_1', 'Label_42'). Use GMAIL_LIST_LABELS to discover available label IDs. Accepts either a list or a JSON-encoded string. Note: If a label appears in both add_label_ids and remove_label_ids, the add operation takes priority. Use GMAIL_CREATE_LABEL first if the label does not yet exist, then supply its returned ID here.

inputs.name: remove_label_ids
inputs.description: List of label IDs to remove from the thread. Must be valid label IDs that exist in the user's account. System labels use uppercase names (e.g., 'INBOX', 'STARRED', 'IMPORTANT', 'UNREAD', 'SPAM', 'TRASH', 'SENT', 'DRAFT', 'CATEGORY_PERSONAL', 'CATEGORY_SOCIAL', 'CATEGORY_PROMOTIONS', 'CATEGORY_UPDATES', 'CATEGORY_FORUMS'). Custom labels use the format 'Label_N' (e.g., 'Label_1', 'Label_42'). Use GMAIL_LIST_LABELS to discover available label IDs. Accepts either a list or a JSON-encoded string. Note: Labels that appear in both add_label_ids and remove_label_ids will be automatically removed from this list (add takes priority).

requiredInputs.name: thread_id
requiredInputs.description: Immutable ID of the thread to modify.

optionalInputs.name: user_id
optionalInputs.description: User's email address or 'me' for the authenticated user.

optionalInputs.name: add_label_ids
optionalInputs.description: List of label IDs to add to the thread. Must be valid label IDs that exist in the user's account. System labels use uppercase names (e.g., 'INBOX', 'STARRED', 'IMPORTANT', 'UNREAD', 'SPAM', 'TRASH', 'SENT', 'DRAFT', 'CATEGORY_PERSONAL', 'CATEGORY_SOCIAL', 'CATEGORY_PROMOTIONS', 'CATEGORY_UPDATES', 'CATEGORY_FORUMS'). Custom labels use the format 'Label_N' (e.g., 'Label_1', 'Label_42'). Use GMAIL_LIST_LABELS to discover available label IDs. Accepts either a list or a JSON-encoded string. Note: If a label appears in both add_label_ids and remove_label_ids, the add operation takes priority. Use GMAIL_CREATE_LABEL first if the label does not yet exist, then supply its returned ID here.

optionalInputs.name: remove_label_ids
optionalInputs.description: List of label IDs to remove from the thread. Must be valid label IDs that exist in the user's account. System labels use uppercase names (e.g., 'INBOX', 'STARRED', 'IMPORTANT', 'UNREAD', 'SPAM', 'TRASH', 'SENT', 'DRAFT', 'CATEGORY_PERSONAL', 'CATEGORY_SOCIAL', 'CATEGORY_PROMOTIONS', 'CATEGORY_UPDATES', 'CATEGORY_FORUMS'). Custom labels use the format 'Label_N' (e.g., 'Label_1', 'Label_42'). Use GMAIL_LIST_LABELS to discover available label IDs. Accepts either a list or a JSON-encoded string. Note: Labels that appear in both add_label_ids and remove_label_ids will be automatically removed from this list (add takes priority).

outputs.description: Data from the action execution

name: Move File
description: Tool to move a file from one folder to another in Google Drive. To truly move (not just copy the parent), always provide both `add_parents` (destination folder ID) and `remove_parents` (source folder ID); omitting `remove_parents` leaves the file in multiple folders. Useful for reorganizing files, including newly created Google Docs/Sheets that default to Drive root.

inputs.name: file_id
inputs.description: The ID of the file to move. Must be a non-empty string.

inputs.name: add_parents
inputs.description: The ID of the single destination folder (e.g., '1FmTIJYwTENUDXOKyNJp7OmcRBvP_6DmT'). Must be a valid Google Drive folder ID consisting of alphanumeric characters, hyphens, and underscores. Folder names are not accepted.

inputs.name: ocr_language
inputs.description: A language hint for OCR processing during image import (ISO 639-1 code).

inputs.name: include_labels
inputs.description: A comma-separated list of IDs of labels to include in the `labelInfo` part of the response.

inputs.name: remove_parents
inputs.description: A comma-separated list of parent folder IDs to remove the file from. Use this to specify the source folder.

inputs.name: supports_all_drives
inputs.description: Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives. Set to true if moving files to or from a shared drive.

inputs.name: keep_revision_forever
inputs.description: Whether to set the 'keepForever' field in the new head revision. This is only applicable to files with binary content in Google Drive.

inputs.name: include_permissions_for_view
inputs.description: Specifies which additional view's permissions to include in the response. Only 'published' is supported.

inputs.name: use_content_as_indexable_text
inputs.description: Whether to use the uploaded content as indexable text.

requiredInputs.name: file_id
requiredInputs.description: The ID of the file to move. Must be a non-empty string.

optionalInputs.name: add_parents
optionalInputs.description: The ID of the single destination folder (e.g., '1FmTIJYwTENUDXOKyNJp7OmcRBvP_6DmT'). Must be a valid Google Drive folder ID consisting of alphanumeric characters, hyphens, and underscores. Folder names are not accepted.

optionalInputs.name: ocr_language
optionalInputs.description: A language hint for OCR processing during image import (ISO 639-1 code).

optionalInputs.name: include_labels
optionalInputs.description: A comma-separated list of IDs of labels to include in the `labelInfo` part of the response.

optionalInputs.name: remove_parents
optionalInputs.description: A comma-separated list of parent folder IDs to remove the file from. Use this to specify the source folder.

optionalInputs.name: supports_all_drives
optionalInputs.description: Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives. Set to true if moving files to or from a shared drive.

optionalInputs.name: keep_revision_forever
optionalInputs.description: Whether to set the 'keepForever' field in the new head revision. This is only applicable to files with binary content in Google Drive.

optionalInputs.name: include_permissions_for_view
optionalInputs.description: Specifies which additional view's permissions to include in the response. Only 'published' is supported.

optionalInputs.name: use_content_as_indexable_text
optionalInputs.description: Whether to use the uploaded content as indexable text.

outputs.name: data
outputs.description: Data from the action execution

outputs.name: error
outputs.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

outputs.name: successful
outputs.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

name: Move Task
description: Moves the specified task to another position in the task list or to a different task list. Use cases: - Reorder tasks within a list (use 'previous' parameter) - Create subtasks by moving a task under a parent (use 'parent' parameter) - Move tasks between different task lists (use 'destinationTasklist' parameter) - Move a subtask back to top-level (omit 'parent' parameter)

inputs.name: task
inputs.description: Task identifier of the task to move. Must be a valid task ID from the source tasklist. Obtain this from 'List Tasks' or 'Insert Task' actions.

inputs.name: parent
inputs.description: New parent task identifier. If provided, the task becomes a subtask of this parent. The parent must exist in the destination list (or source list if no destinationTasklist is specified). Omit to move task to top level.

inputs.name: previous
inputs.description: New previous sibling task identifier. The task will be positioned immediately after this sibling. Omit to position the task first among its siblings. Must exist in the same list as the task's destination (destinationTasklist if specified, otherwise source tasklist). Cross-list sibling references are unsupported.

inputs.name: tasklist
inputs.description: Source task list identifier where the task currently exists. Use '@default' for the user's primary task list, or provide a specific task list ID obtained from 'List Task Lists'. The task must exist in this list.

inputs.name: destinationTasklist
inputs.description: Destination task list identifier. If set, the task is moved from the source tasklist to this list. Omit to reorder within the same list. Note: Recurrent tasks cannot be moved between lists.

requiredInputs.name: task
requiredInputs.description: Task identifier of the task to move. Must be a valid task ID from the source tasklist. Obtain this from 'List Tasks' or 'Insert Task' actions.

requiredInputs.name: tasklist
requiredInputs.description: Source task list identifier where the task currently exists. Use '@default' for the user's primary task list, or provide a specific task list ID obtained from 'List Task Lists'. The task must exist in this list.

optionalInputs.name: parent
optionalInputs.description: New parent task identifier. If provided, the task becomes a subtask of this parent. The parent must exist in the destination list (or source list if no destinationTasklist is specified). Omit to move task to top level.

optionalInputs.name: previous
optionalInputs.description: New previous sibling task identifier. The task will be positioned immediately after this sibling. Omit to position the task first among its siblings. Must exist in the same list as the task's destination (destinationTasklist if specified, otherwise source tasklist). Cross-list sibling references are unsupported.

optionalInputs.name: destinationTasklist
optionalInputs.description: Destination task list identifier. If set, the task is moved from the source tasklist to this list. Omit to reorder within the same list. Note: Recurrent tasks cannot be moved between lists.

outputs.name: data
outputs.description: Data from the action execution

outputs.name: error
outputs.description: Error if any occurred during the execution of the action

outputs.name: successful
outputs.description: Whether or not the action execution was successful or not

name: Trash thread
description: Moves the specified thread to the trash. Any messages that belong to the thread are also moved to the trash.

inputs.name: user_id
inputs.description: The user's email address. The special value 'me' can be used to indicate the authenticated user.

inputs.name: thread_id
inputs.description: Required. The ID of the thread to trash. This moves all messages in the thread to trash.

requiredInputs.name: thread_id
requiredInputs.description: Required. The ID of the thread to trash. This moves all messages in the thread to trash.

name: Move to Trash
description: Moves an existing, non-deleted email message to the trash for the specified user. Trashed messages are recoverable and still count toward storage quota until purged. Prefer this over GMAIL_BATCH_DELETE_MESSAGES when recovery may be needed. For bulk operations, use GMAIL_BATCH_MODIFY_MESSAGES or GMAIL_BATCH_DELETE_MESSAGES instead of repeated calls to this tool.

inputs.name: user_id
inputs.description: User's email address or 'me' for the authenticated user.

inputs.name: message_id
inputs.description: Required. The unique identifier of the email message to move to trash. This is a hexadecimal string that can be obtained from listing or fetching emails. Verify the correct message via subject/snippet before trashing to avoid affecting unrelated conversations.

name: Mutate Ad Groups
description: Create, update, or remove ad groups within Google Ads campaigns. Supports batch operations with multiple ad group changes in a single request. name: Mutate Campaigns
description: Create, update, or remove Google Ads campaigns in batch. Supports multiple operations (create, update, remove) in a single request. Use when managing campaign lifecycle, applying bulk changes, or automating campaign management workflows. This action is irreversible for remove operations — deleted campaigns cannot be recovered. Plan accordingly and consider using validate_only=true to test changes before applying them.

inputs.description: List of campaign operations to perform. Each operation can be create, update, or remove. At least one operation is required.

inputs.name: create
inputs.description: Campaign data for create or update operations.

inputs.name: status
inputs.description: Status of the campaign.

inputs.name: end_date

inputs.name: manual_cpc

inputs.name: start_date
inputs.description: Start date of the campaign in YYYY-MM-DD format.

inputs.name: daily_budget
inputs.description: Daily budget amount in the account's currency.

inputs.name: network_type
inputs.description: Network settings for the campaign (e.g., 'google_search', 'youtube').

inputs.name: resource_name
inputs.description: Resource name of the campaign for update operations. Format: customers/{customer_id}/campaigns/{campaign_id}

inputs.name: campaign_budget

name: Mutate conditional format rules
description: Add, update, delete, or reorder conditional format rules on a Google Sheet. Use when you need to create, modify, or remove conditional formatting without manually building batchUpdate requests. Supports four operations: ADD (create new rule), UPDATE (replace existing rule), DELETE (remove rule), MOVE (reorder rules by changing index).

inputs.name: rule
inputs.description: Conditional format rule specification.

inputs.name: ranges
inputs.description: Ranges where formatting applies (must be on same sheet).

inputs.name: sheetId
inputs.description: The sheet ID containing this range.

inputs.name: endRowIndex
inputs.description: The end row (exclusive), 0-indexed.

inputs.name: startRowIndex
inputs.description: The start row (inclusive), 0-indexed.

inputs.name: endColumnIndex
inputs.description: The end column (exclusive), 0-indexed.

inputs.name: startColumnIndex
inputs.description: The start column (inclusive), 0-indexed.

inputs.name: booleanRule
inputs.description: Boolean rule for conditional formatting.

inputs.name: format
inputs.description: Formatting to apply when condition is true.

inputs.name: textFormat
inputs.description:
Text formatting options for conditional formatting.

IMPORTANT: Only bold, italic, strikethrough, and foreground color are supported
in conditional formatting. Fields like fontSize, fontFamily, and underline are NOT
supported and will cause API errors if included.

inputs.name: type
inputs.description: Condition type. Valid values: NUMBER_GREATER, NUMBER_GREATER_THAN_EQ, NUMBER_LESS, NUMBER_LESS_THAN_EQ, NUMBER_EQ, NUMBER_NOT_EQ, NUMBER_BETWEEN, NUMBER_NOT_BETWEEN, TEXT_CONTAINS, TEXT_NOT_CONTAINS, TEXT_STARTS_WITH, TEXT_ENDS_WITH, TEXT_EQ, TEXT_NOT_EQ, TEXT_IS_EMAIL, TEXT_IS_URL, DATE_EQ, DATE_BEFORE, DATE_AFTER, DATE_ON_OR_BEFORE, DATE_ON_OR_AFTER, DATE_BETWEEN, DATE_NOT_BETWEEN, DATE_NOT_EQ, DATE_IS_VALID, ONE_OF_RANGE, ONE_OF_LIST, BLANK, NOT_BLANK, CUSTOM_FORMULA, BOOLEAN, FILTER_EXPRESSION.

inputs.name: userEnteredValue
inputs.description: Value as entered by user (formula, number, or text). Always provide as a string, even for numeric values (e.g., '0.01' not 0.01). For CUSTOM_FORMULA conditions: formulas must begin with '=' or '+'; the formula must evaluate to true/false; same-sheet references work normally (e.g., '=A1>100'); cross-sheet references require INDIRECT function (use '=COUNTIF(INDIRECT("Sheet2!A:A"),A1)>0' instead of '=COUNTIF(Sheet2!A:A,A1)>0'). name: Nearby search
description: Searches for places (e.g., restaurants, parks) within a specified circular area, with options to filter by place types and customize the returned fields and number of results.

inputs.name: radius
inputs.description: Radius of the circular search area in meters.

inputs.name: latitude
inputs.description: Latitude coordinate of the search center in decimal degrees.

inputs.name: fieldMask
inputs.description: Comma-separated list of place fields for the response. Fields are automatically prefixed with 'places.' if missing. Common fields: displayName, formattedAddress, id, types, rating, userRatingCount, location, businessStatus, regularOpeningHours, photos, reviews. Use '*' for all fields (not recommended for production). Valid field names include: displayName, formattedAddress, shortFormattedAddress, id, types, primaryType, primaryTypeDisplayName, location, viewport, plusCode, rating, userRatingCount, reviews, regularOpeningHours, currentOpeningHours, photos, websiteUri, googleMapsUri, businessStatus, priceLevel, priceRange, addressComponents, utcOffsetMinutes, internationalPhoneNumber, nationalPhoneNumber, editorialSummary, accessibilityOptions, parkingOptions, paymentOptions, delivery, dineIn, takeout, reservable, servesBreakfast, servesLunch, servesDinner, servesBeer, servesWine, allowsDogs, outdoorSeating, liveMusic, goodForChildren, goodForGroups.

inputs.name: longitude
inputs.description: Longitude coordinate of the search center in decimal degrees.

inputs.name: excludedTypes
inputs.description: List of place types to exclude from Table A. Results matching any of these types are omitted. Up to 50 types allowed. Must be provided as a list, e.g., ['cafe'] for a single type or ['cafe', 'store'] for multiple types. Must use valid Table A types only.

inputs.name: includedTypes
inputs.description: List of place types to include from Table A. Results will match at least one of these types. Up to 50 types allowed. Must be provided as a list, e.g., ['restaurant'] for a single type or ['atm', 'bank'] for multiple types. Common types: restaurant, cafe, bank, atm, hospital, pharmacy, school, park, gym, hotel, airport, gas_station. IMPORTANT: 'food' is NOT a valid type (it's in Table B); use specific types like restaurant, cafe, bakery, fast_food_restaurant instead. Note: 'locality' is valid, but 'city', 'town', 'sublocality', 'landmark', and 'point_of_interest' are NOT valid for filtering.

inputs.name: maxResultCount
inputs.description: Maximum number of search results to return. Valid range: 1-20. The actual count may be lower based on available places.

requiredInputs.name: radius
requiredInputs.description: Radius of the circular search area in meters.

requiredInputs.name: latitude
requiredInputs.description: Latitude coordinate of the search center in decimal degrees.

requiredInputs.name: longitude
requiredInputs.description: Longitude coordinate of the search center in decimal degrees.

optionalInputs.name: fieldMask
optionalInputs.description: Comma-separated list of place fields for the response. Fields are automatically prefixed with 'places.' if missing. Common fields: displayName, formattedAddress, id, types, rating, userRatingCount, location, businessStatus, regularOpeningHours, photos, reviews. Use '*' for all fields (not recommended for production). Valid field names include: displayName, formattedAddress, shortFormattedAddress, id, types, primaryType, primaryTypeDisplayName, location, viewport, plusCode, rating, userRatingCount, reviews, regularOpeningHours, currentOpeningHours, photos, websiteUri, googleMapsUri, businessStatus, priceLevel, priceRange, addressComponents, utcOffsetMinutes, internationalPhoneNumber, nationalPhoneNumber, editorialSummary, accessibilityOptions, parkingOptions, paymentOptions, delivery, dineIn, takeout, reservable, servesBreakfast, servesLunch, servesDinner, servesBeer, servesWine, allowsDogs, outdoorSeating, liveMusic, goodForChildren, goodForGroups.

optionalInputs.name: excludedTypes
optionalInputs.description: List of place types to exclude from Table A. Results matching any of these types are omitted. Up to 50 types allowed. Must be provided as a list, e.g., ['cafe'] for a single type or ['cafe', 'store'] for multiple types. Must use valid Table A types only.

optionalInputs.name: includedTypes
optionalInputs.description: List of place types to include from Table A. Results will match at least one of these types. Up to 50 types allowed. Must be provided as a list, e.g., ['restaurant'] for a single type or ['atm', 'bank'] for multiple types. Common types: restaurant, cafe, bank, atm, hospital, pharmacy, school, park, gym, hotel, airport, gas_station. IMPORTANT: 'food' is NOT a valid type (it's in Table B); use specific types like restaurant, cafe, bakery, fast_food_restaurant instead. Note: 'locality' is valid, but 'city', 'town', 'sublocality', 'landmark', and 'point_of_interest' are NOT valid for filtering.

name: Export or download a file
description: DEPRECATED: Exports Google Workspace files (max 10MB) to a specified format using `mime_type`, or downloads other file types; use `GOOGLEDRIVE_DOWNLOAD_FILE` instead.

inputs.name: file_id
inputs.description: The unique ID of the file stored in Google Drive that you want to export or download.

inputs.name: mime_type
inputs.description: Target MIME type for exporting Google Workspace files only. Supported exports by source type: Google Docs -> DOCX, ODT, RTF, PDF, TXT, ZIP (HTML), EPUB, MD; Google Sheets -> XLSX, ODS, PDF, ZIP (HTML), CSV, TSV; Google Slides -> PPTX, ODP, PDF, TXT, JPG, PNG, SVG; Google Drawings -> PDF, JPG, PNG, SVG; Apps Script -> JSON. If omitted, a default format is used: Docs->PDF, Sheets->XLSX, Slides->PDF, Drawings->PDF. For non-Workspace files (PDFs, images, text files, etc.), this parameter is ignored and the file is downloaded in its native format.

requiredInputs.name: file_id
requiredInputs.description: The unique ID of the file stored in Google Drive that you want to export or download.

optionalInputs.name: mime_type
optionalInputs.description: Target MIME type for exporting Google Workspace files only. Supported exports by source type: Google Docs -> DOCX, ODT, RTF, PDF, TXT, ZIP (HTML), EPUB, MD; Google Sheets -> XLSX, ODS, PDF, ZIP (HTML), CSV, TSV; Google Slides -> PPTX, ODP, PDF, TXT, JPG, PNG, SVG; Google Drawings -> PDF, JPG, PNG, SVG; Apps Script -> JSON. If omitted, a default format is used: Docs->PDF, Sheets->XLSX, Slides->PDF, Drawings->PDF. For non-Workspace files (PDFs, images, text files, etc.), this parameter is ignored and the file is downloaded in its native format.

name: Patch Calendar
description: Partially updates (PATCHes) an existing Google Calendar, modifying only the fields provided. At least one of summary, description, location, or timezone must be provided. Empty strings for `description` or `location` clear them.

inputs.name: summary
inputs.description: New title for the calendar; cannot be an empty string when provided. At least one of summary, description, location, or timezone must be provided.

inputs.name: location
inputs.description: New geographic location of the calendar (e.g., 'Paris, France').

inputs.name: timezone
inputs.description: New IANA Time Zone Database name for the calendar (e.g., 'Europe/Zurich', 'America/New_York'). Calendars duplicated via GOOGLECALENDAR_DUPLICATE_CALENDAR may default to UTC; set the correct timezone explicitly after duplication.

inputs.name: calendar_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the Google Calendar to update. Use 'primary' for the main calendar, or a calendar's unique ID (typically in email format like 'abc123@group.calendar.google.com'). IMPORTANT: This is NOT the calendar's display name/title - use GOOGLECALENDAR_LIST_CALENDARS to find the 'id' field for a calendar.

inputs.name: description
inputs.description: New description for the calendar.

requiredInputs.name: calendar_id
requiredInputs.description: The unique identifier of the Google Calendar to update. Use 'primary' for the main calendar, or a calendar's unique ID (typically in email format like 'abc123@group.calendar.google.com'). IMPORTANT: This is NOT the calendar's display name/title - use GOOGLECALENDAR_LIST_CALENDARS to find the 'id' field for a calendar.

optionalInputs.name: summary
optionalInputs.description: New title for the calendar; cannot be an empty string when provided. At least one of summary, description, location, or timezone must be provided.

optionalInputs.name: location
optionalInputs.description: New geographic location of the calendar (e.g., 'Paris, France').

optionalInputs.name: description
optionalInputs.description: New description for the calendar.

name: Patch Event
description: Update specified fields of an existing event in a Google Calendar using patch semantics (array fields like `attendees` are fully replaced if provided); ensure the `calendar_id` and `event_id` are valid and the user has write access to the calendar.

inputs.name: source
inputs.description: Input model for event source.

inputs.name: url
inputs.description: Source URL

inputs.name: title
inputs.description: Source title

inputs.name: status
inputs.description: Status of the event.

inputs.name: summary
inputs.description: New title for the event.

inputs.name: color_id
inputs.description: Color ID for the event (1-11). Use GOOGLECALENDAR_COLORS_GET to retrieve available colors.

inputs.name: end_time
inputs.description: New end time (RFC3339 timestamp, e.g., '2024-07-01T11:00:00-07:00'). Uses `timezone` if provided, otherwise UTC. For all-day events, use YYYY-MM-DD format (exclusive end date). Optional when updating start_time - the original event duration will be preserved if end_time is not specified. Must be strictly after `start_time`; mismatched or mixed formats cause HTTP 400 `timeRangeEmpty`.

inputs.name: event_id
inputs.description: The unique technical identifier of the event to update. IMPORTANT: This is NOT the event title/name. Event IDs are opaque strings typically base32hex encoded (5-1024 characters using lowercase a-v and digits 0-9). For recurring event instances, the ID format is 'baseEventId_YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ' with an underscore separator (e.g., 'abc123def456_20260115T100000Z'). To get an event ID, first use GOOGLECALENDAR_FIND_EVENT or GOOGLECALENDAR_EVENTS_LIST to search for events and retrieve their IDs. Use master event ID to update the entire recurring series; use instance ID to update only that occurrence — confirm scope before patching.

inputs.name: location
inputs.description: New geographic location (physical address or virtual meeting link).

inputs.name: sequence
inputs.description: Sequence number as per iCalendar specification. Incremented on each event update.

inputs.name: timezone
inputs.description: IANA Time Zone Database name for start/end times (e.g., 'America/Los_Angeles'). Used if `start_time` and `end_time` are provided and not all-day dates; defaults to UTC if unset. Mismatched or missing timezone with offset timestamps silently shifts event to unintended wall-clock time.

inputs.name: attendees
inputs.description: List of valid email addresses for attendees (e.g., 'user@example.com'). Replaces existing attendees. Provide an empty list to remove all.

inputs.name: reminders
inputs.description: Input model for reminders.

inputs.name: overrides
inputs.description: Override reminders (max 5)

inputs.name: method
inputs.description: Reminder method

inputs.name: minutes
inputs.description: Minutes before start to trigger

inputs.name: useDefault
inputs.description: Whether to use default calendar reminders

inputs.name: recurrence
inputs.description: RRULE, EXRULE, RDATE and EXDATE lines per RFC5545 for recurring events. Replaces existing recurrence rules if provided.

inputs.name: start_time
inputs.description: New start time (RFC3339 timestamp, e.g., '2024-07-01T10:00:00-07:00'). Uses `timezone` if provided, otherwise UTC. For all-day events, use YYYY-MM-DD format. When only start_time is provided without end_time, the event's original duration is preserved automatically.

inputs.name: visibility
inputs.description: Visibility of the event.

inputs.name: attachments
inputs.description: File attachments (max 25). Each with 'fileUrl' (required) and optional 'title', 'mimeType'. Requires supportsAttachments=true.

inputs.name: title
inputs.description: Title of the attachment

inputs.name: fileUrl
inputs.description: URL of the file attachment

inputs.name: mimeType
inputs.description: MIME type of the attachment

inputs.name: calendar_id
inputs.description: Identifier of the calendar. Use 'primary' for the primary calendar of the logged-in user. To find other calendar IDs, use the `calendarList.list` method. Must be provided in snake_case format.

inputs.name: description
inputs.description: New description for the event; can include HTML.

inputs.name: send_updates
inputs.description: Whether to send update notifications to attendees. Uses default user behavior if unspecified. Set explicitly to avoid unintended notifications when removing attendees or making bulk updates.

inputs.name: transparency
inputs.description: Whether the event blocks time on the calendar. 'opaque' (default) blocks time, 'transparent' does not.

inputs.name: max_attendees
inputs.description: Maximum attendees in response; does not affect invited count. If more, response includes organizer only. Must be positive.

inputs.name: rsvp_response
inputs.description: RSVP response status for the authenticated user. Updates only the current user's response status without affecting other attendees. Note: RSVP is only supported for regular calendar events (eventType='default'); attempting to RSVP to focusTime, outOfOffice, birthday, or workingLocation events will result in an error.

requiredInputs.name: event_id
requiredInputs.description: The unique technical identifier of the event to update. IMPORTANT: This is NOT the event title/name. Event IDs are opaque strings typically base32hex encoded (5-1024 characters using lowercase a-v and digits 0-9). For recurring event instances, the ID format is 'baseEventId_YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ' with an underscore separator (e.g., 'abc123def456_20260115T100000Z'). To get an event ID, first use GOOGLECALENDAR_FIND_EVENT or GOOGLECALENDAR_EVENTS_LIST to search for events and retrieve their IDs. Use master event ID to update the entire recurring series; use instance ID to update only that occurrence — confirm scope before patching.

requiredInputs.name: calendar_id
requiredInputs.description: Identifier of the calendar. Use 'primary' for the primary calendar of the logged-in user. To find other calendar IDs, use the `calendarList.list` method. Must be provided in snake_case format.

optionalInputs.description: Color ID for the event (1-11). Use GOOGLECALENDAR_COLORS_GET to retrieve available colors.

optionalInputs.name: end_time
optionalInputs.description: New end time (RFC3339 timestamp, e.g., '2024-07-01T11:00:00-07:00'). Uses `timezone` if provided, otherwise UTC. For all-day events, use YYYY-MM-DD format (exclusive end date). Optional when updating start_time - the original event duration will be preserved if end_time is not specified. Must be strictly after `start_time`; mismatched or mixed formats cause HTTP 400 `timeRangeEmpty`.

optionalInputs.name: location
optionalInputs.description: New geographic location (physical address or virtual meeting link).

optionalInputs.name: sequence
optionalInputs.description: Sequence number as per iCalendar specification. Incremented on each event update.

optionalInputs.name: timezone
optionalInputs.description: IANA Time Zone Database name for start/end times (e.g., 'America/Los_Angeles'). Used if `start_time` and `end_time` are provided and not all-day dates; defaults to UTC if unset. Mismatched or missing timezone with offset timestamps silently shifts event to unintended wall-clock time.

optionalInputs.name: attendees
optionalInputs.description: List of valid email addresses for attendees (e.g., 'user@example.com'). Replaces existing attendees. Provide an empty list to remove all.

optionalInputs.description: Input model for reminders.

optionalInputs.description: Override reminders (max 5)

optionalInputs.name: method
optionalInputs.description: Reminder method

optionalInputs.name: minutes
optionalInputs.description: Minutes before start to trigger

optionalInputs.name: useDefault
optionalInputs.description: Whether to use default calendar reminders

optionalInputs.name: recurrence
optionalInputs.description: RRULE, EXRULE, RDATE and EXDATE lines per RFC5545 for recurring events. Replaces existing recurrence rules if provided.

optionalInputs.name: start_time
optionalInputs.description: New start time (RFC3339 timestamp, e.g., '2024-07-01T10:00:00-07:00'). Uses `timezone` if provided, otherwise UTC. For all-day events, use YYYY-MM-DD format. When only start_time is provided without end_time, the event's original duration is preserved automatically.

optionalInputs.name: visibility
optionalInputs.description: Visibility of the event.

optionalInputs.name: attachments
optionalInputs.description: File attachments (max 25). Each with 'fileUrl' (required) and optional 'title', 'mimeType'. Requires supportsAttachments=true.

optionalInputs.name: title
optionalInputs.description: Title of the attachment

optionalInputs.name: fileUrl
optionalInputs.description: URL of the file attachment

optionalInputs.name: mimeType
optionalInputs.description: MIME type of the attachment

optionalInputs.name: description
optionalInputs.description: New description for the event; can include HTML.

optionalInputs.name: send_updates
optionalInputs.description: Whether to send update notifications to attendees. Uses default user behavior if unspecified. Set explicitly to avoid unintended notifications when removing attendees or making bulk updates.

optionalInputs.name: transparency
optionalInputs.description: Whether the event blocks time on the calendar. 'opaque' (default) blocks time, 'transparent' does not.

optionalInputs.name: max_attendees
optionalInputs.description: Maximum attendees in response; does not affect invited count. If more, response includes organizer only. Must be positive.

name: Patch Label
description: Patches the specified user-created label. System labels (e.g., INBOX, SENT, SPAM) cannot be modified and will be rejected.

inputs.name: id
inputs.description: The ID of the label to update.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The display name of the label. At least one of 'name', 'messageListVisibility', 'labelListVisibility', or 'color' must be provided. Must be non-empty, unique among user labels, and must not contain `,`, `/`, or `.`.

inputs.name: color
inputs.description: The color to assign to the label. Color is only available for labels that have their `type` set to `user`. At least one of 'name', 'messageListVisibility', 'labelListVisibility', or 'color' must be provided. Must include both `backgroundColor` and `textColor` subfields; both values must come from Gmail's predefined color palette — arbitrary hex values or omitting either field causes a 400 error.

inputs.name: textColor
inputs.description: The text color of the label, represented as a hex string. Must be one of Gmail's predefined colors from the color palette. See: https://developers.google.com/workspace/gmail/api/guides/labels#color_palette

inputs.name: backgroundColor
inputs.description: The background color of the label, represented as a hex string. Must be one of Gmail's predefined colors from the color palette. See: https://developers.google.com/workspace/gmail/api/guides/labels#color_palette

inputs.name: userId
inputs.description: The user's email address. The special value `me` can be used to indicate the authenticated user.

inputs.name: labelListVisibility
inputs.description: The visibility of the label in the label list in the Gmail web interface. At least one of 'name', 'messageListVisibility', 'labelListVisibility', or 'color' must be provided.

inputs.name: messageListVisibility
inputs.description: The visibility of messages with this label in the message list in the Gmail web interface. At least one of 'name', 'messageListVisibility', 'labelListVisibility', or 'color' must be provided.

requiredInputs.name: id
requiredInputs.description: The ID of the label to update.

requiredInputs.name: userId
requiredInputs.description: The user's email address. The special value `me` can be used to indicate the authenticated user.

optionalInputs.description: The display name of the label. At least one of 'name', 'messageListVisibility', 'labelListVisibility', or 'color' must be provided. Must be non-empty, unique among user labels, and must not contain `,`, `/`, or `.`.

optionalInputs.name: color
optionalInputs.description: The color to assign to the label. Color is only available for labels that have their `type` set to `user`. At least one of 'name', 'messageListVisibility', 'labelListVisibility', or 'color' must be provided. Must include both `backgroundColor` and `textColor` subfields; both values must come from Gmail's predefined color palette — arbitrary hex values or omitting either field causes a 400 error.

optionalInputs.name: textColor
optionalInputs.description: The text color of the label, represented as a hex string. Must be one of Gmail's predefined colors from the color palette. See: https://developers.google.com/workspace/gmail/api/guides/labels#color_palette

name: Patch Permission
description: Tool to update a permission using patch semantics. Use when you need to modify specific fields of an existing permission without affecting other fields. **Warning:** Concurrent permissions operations on the same file are not supported; only the last update is applied.

inputs.name: role
inputs.description: Permission roles that can be granted in Google Drive.

inputs.name: file_id
inputs.description: The ID for the file or shared drive.

inputs.name: with_link
inputs.description: Whether the link is required for this permission. Set to true for 'anyone with the link' access (not publicly discoverable), or false for publicly discoverable access.

inputs.name: permission_id
inputs.description: The ID for the permission. Use 'anyone' for public link permissions, or specific permission IDs for user/group/domain permissions. You can get permission IDs by calling GOOGLEDRIVE_LIST_PERMISSIONS.

inputs.name: expiration_date
inputs.description: The time at which this permission will expire (RFC 3339 date-time). Can only be set on user and group permissions. The date must be in the future and cannot be more than a year in the future.

inputs.name: additional_roles
inputs.description: Additional roles for this user. Only 'commenter' is currently allowed.

inputs.name: remove_expiration
inputs.description: Whether to remove the expiration date. Set to true to make the permission permanent.

inputs.name: transfer_ownership
inputs.description: Whether changing a role to 'owner' downgrades the current owners to writers. Does nothing if the specified role is not 'owner'. Required as an acknowledgement when transferring ownership.

inputs.name: supports_all_drives
inputs.description: Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives.

inputs.name: use_domain_admin_access
inputs.description: Issue the request as a domain administrator; if set to true, then the requester will be granted access if the file ID parameter refers to a shared drive and the requester is an administrator of the domain to which the shared drive belongs.

requiredInputs.name: file_id
requiredInputs.description: The ID for the file or shared drive.

requiredInputs.name: permission_id
requiredInputs.description: The ID for the permission. Use 'anyone' for public link permissions, or specific permission IDs for user/group/domain permissions. You can get permission IDs by calling GOOGLEDRIVE_LIST_PERMISSIONS.

optionalInputs.name: role
optionalInputs.description: Permission roles that can be granted in Google Drive.

optionalInputs.name: with_link
optionalInputs.description: Whether the link is required for this permission. Set to true for 'anyone with the link' access (not publicly discoverable), or false for publicly discoverable access.

optionalInputs.name: expiration_date
optionalInputs.description: The time at which this permission will expire (RFC 3339 date-time). Can only be set on user and group permissions. The date must be in the future and cannot be more than a year in the future.

optionalInputs.name: additional_roles
optionalInputs.description: Additional roles for this user. Only 'commenter' is currently allowed.

optionalInputs.name: remove_expiration
optionalInputs.description: Whether to remove the expiration date. Set to true to make the permission permanent.

name: Patch send-as alias
description: Tool to patch the specified send-as alias for a Gmail user. Use when you need to update properties of an existing send-as email address such as display name, reply-to address, signature, default status, or SMTP configuration.

inputs.name: user_id
inputs.description: The user's email address or the special value 'me' to indicate the authenticated user.

inputs.name: smtp_msa
inputs.description: Configuration for SMTP relay service.

inputs.name: host
inputs.description: The hostname of the SMTP service. Required when configuring SMTP.

inputs.name: port
inputs.description: The port of the SMTP service. Required when configuring SMTP.

inputs.name: password
inputs.description: The password for SMTP authentication. This is write-only and never appears in responses.

inputs.name: username
inputs.description: The username for SMTP authentication. This is write-only and never appears in responses.

inputs.name: securityMode
inputs.description: The protocol that will be used to secure communication with the SMTP service. Required when configuring SMTP.

inputs.name: signature
inputs.description: An optional HTML signature that is included in messages composed with this alias in the Gmail web UI. This signature is added to new emails only.

inputs.name: is_default
inputs.description: Whether this address is selected as the default 'From:' address in situations such as composing a new message or sending a vacation auto-reply. Setting this to true will make other send-as addresses non-default. Only true can be written to this field.

inputs.name: display_name
inputs.description: A name that appears in the 'From:' header for mail sent using this alias. For custom 'from' addresses, when empty, Gmail will populate the 'From:' header with the name used for the primary address. If the admin has disabled name updates, requests to update this field for the primary login will silently fail.

inputs.name: send_as_email
inputs.description: The send-as alias email address to update. This is the email address that appears in the 'From:' header.

inputs.name: treat_as_alias
inputs.description: Whether Gmail should treat this address as an alias for the user's primary email address. This setting only applies to custom 'from' aliases.

inputs.name: reply_to_address
inputs.description: An optional email address that is included in a 'Reply-To:' header for mail sent using this alias. If empty, Gmail will not generate a 'Reply-To:' header.

requiredInputs.name: send_as_email
requiredInputs.description: The send-as alias email address to update. This is the email address that appears in the 'From:' header.

optionalInputs.name: user_id

name: Patch Task
description: Partially updates an existing task (identified by `task_id`) within a specific Google Task list (identified by `tasklist_id`), modifying only the provided attributes from `TaskInput` (e.g., `title`, `notes`, `due` date, `status`) and requiring both the task and list to exist.

inputs.name: id
inputs.description: Unique identifier for the task. Generated by Google Tasks API when creating a task. Used for updating or deleting existing tasks.

inputs.name: due
inputs.description: Due date for the task (optional). NOTE: Google Tasks only stores the date portion (YYYY-MM-DD) - any time component provided will be automatically stripped before sending to the API. Accepts RFC3339 format (e.g., '2025-09-28T23:59:00Z') or various human-readable formats like '28 Sep 2025', '11:59 PM, 22 Sep 2025'. Normalized to midnight UTC — naive local dates without timezone offset may shift ±1 day. Time-based reminders are not supported via this field.

inputs.name: etag
inputs.description: ETag of the resource for concurrency control. Prevents accidental overwrites when multiple clients modify the same task. Auto-generated by the API.

inputs.name: notes
inputs.description: Additional details or description for the task. Supports plain text only (no HTML/markdown). Maximum 8192 characters. Example: 'Include Q3 sales data and projections'

inputs.name: title
inputs.description: Title/name of the task. This is the main text displayed for the task. Maximum 1024 characters. Example: 'Complete quarterly report'

inputs.name: hidden
inputs.description: Whether the task is hidden from the default view. Hidden tasks can still be accessed via API but won't show in UI. Default: false

inputs.name: status
inputs.description: Current status of the task. Must be either 'needsAction' (task is pending) or 'completed' (task is done).

inputs.name: deleted
inputs.description: Whether the task has been deleted. Deleted tasks are marked but not immediately removed, allowing for recovery. Default: false

inputs.name: completed
inputs.description: Date/time when the task was marked as completed. Only applicable when status='completed'. Unlike 'due', this field preserves the time component. Accepts RFC3339 format or human-readable date formats. Malformed or non-RFC3339 values will be rejected.

inputs.name: tasklist_id
inputs.description: Identifier of the Google Task list that contains the task to be updated.

requiredInputs.name: task_id
