description: Tool to hide/unhide rows or columns and set row heights or column widths. Use when you need to change visibility or pixel sizing of dimensions in a Google Sheet.

inputs.name: sheet_id
inputs.description: The numeric ID of the sheet (tab). Either sheet_id or sheet_name must be provided. If both are provided, sheet_name will be resolved to sheet_id and override this value.

inputs.name: dimension
inputs.description: Whether to update rows or columns.

inputs.name: end_index
inputs.description: The zero-based end index (exclusive) of the dimension range to update. For example, to update rows 5-9, use start_index=5 and end_index=10.

inputs.name: pixel_size
inputs.description: The height (for rows) or width (for columns) in pixels. Must be a positive integer. At least one of hidden_by_user or pixel_size must be provided.

inputs.name: sheet_name
inputs.description: The name of the sheet (tab). If provided, this will be resolved to the numeric sheet_id using GOOGLESHEETS_GET_SPREADSHEET_INFO. Either sheet_id or sheet_name must be provided.

inputs.name: start_index
inputs.description: The zero-based start index (inclusive) of the dimension range to update. For rows, 0 is the first row; for columns, 0 is column A.

inputs.name: hidden_by_user
inputs.description: Whether to hide (true) or unhide (false) the specified rows/columns. At least one of hidden_by_user or pixel_size must be provided.

inputs.name: spreadsheet_id
inputs.description: The ID of the spreadsheet to update.

inputs.name: response_ranges
inputs.description: Limits the ranges included in the response spreadsheet (only if includeSpreadsheetInResponse is true).

inputs.name: response_include_grid_data
inputs.description: Whether to include grid data in the response (only if includeSpreadsheetInResponse is true).

inputs.name: include_spreadsheet_in_response
inputs.description: Whether to include the updated spreadsheet in the response.

name: Update Document Batch (Deprecated)
description: DEPRECATED: Use UpdateExistingDocument instead. Tool to apply one or more updates to a Google Document. Use when you need to perform batch operations on a document, such as inserting text, updating styles, or modifying document structure. Supports 35+ request types including insertText, replaceAllText, updateTextStyle, createParagraphBullets, insertTable, createHeader/Footer, and more. Each request is validated before being applied. If any request is invalid, the entire operation fails and nothing is applied.

inputs.name: requests
inputs.description: A list of updates to apply to the document. Each request dict must contain exactly one request type key (e.g., insertText, replaceAllText, updateTextStyle, createParagraphBullets). For updateTextStyle, range must be non-empty (startIndex < endIndex). See https://developers.google.com/docs/api/reference/rest/v1/documents/request for all types.

inputs.name: document_id
inputs.description: The ID of the document to update.

inputs.name: write_control
inputs.description: Provides control over how write requests are executed.

inputs.name: targetRevisionId
inputs.description: The optional target revision ID of the document the write request is applied to. If collaborator changes have occurred after the document was read using the API, the changes produced by this write request are applied against the collaborator changes.

inputs.name: requiredRevisionId
inputs.description: The optional revision ID of the document the write request is applied to. If this is not the latest revision of the document, the request is not processed and returns a 400 bad request error.

name: Update Document Markdown
description: Replaces the entire content of an existing Google Docs document with new Markdown text; requires edit permissions for the document.

inputs.name: id
inputs.description: The unique identifier for the Google Docs document to update. Accepts either a document ID (alphanumeric string, length varies by document age - newer documents typically have 44-character IDs) or a full Google Docs URL (e.g., https://docs.google.com/document/d/{DOCUMENT_ID}/edit). When a URL is provided, the document ID will be automatically extracted.

inputs.name: markdown
inputs.description: Markdown content that will replace the document's entire existing content. Supports standard Markdown features.

requiredInputs.name: id

name: Update Document Section Markdown
description: Tool to insert or replace a section of a Google Docs document with Markdown content. Use when you need to update only a section of a document by specifying start and optional end indices. Supports full Markdown formatting.

inputs.name: end_index
inputs.description: Optional one-based end index of the content to replace (exclusive); must be greater than or equal to start_index. If end_index is not provided, the content is inserted at the start_index without replacing existing content. Requires start_index to be provided - if start_index is omitted (append mode), end_index is ignored. Important: Use the exact indices from GOOGLEDOCS_GET_DOCUMENT_BY_ID without modification. The Google Docs API does not allow deleting the last newline character of a document segment (body, header, footer, etc.), so ensure end_index does not include the final segment newline.

inputs.name: document_id
inputs.description: The unique ID of the Google Docs document to update. This is the alphanumeric string found in the document URL: https://docs.google.com/document/d/{DOCUMENT_ID}/edit. Valid IDs are typically 44 characters long and contain only letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores (e.g., '1BxiMVs0XRA5nFMdKvBdBZjgmUUqptlbs74OgvE2upms'). To get a document ID: create a new document using GOOGLEDOCS_CREATE_DOCUMENT, or extract it from an existing document's URL. Also accepts parameter aliases: 'id', 'doc_id', 'documentId', or 'docId'.

inputs.name: start_index
inputs.description: One-based UTF-16 code unit index where to insert or start replacement. If not provided, content will be appended to the end of the document. Use 1 to insert at the very beginning of the document. To insert at a specific position, first use GOOGLEDOCS_GET_DOCUMENT_BY_ID to retrieve the document structure and find the desired index from the body.content elements' start_index/end_index values. If the provided index equals or exceeds the document segment's end index, it will be automatically adjusted to the last valid position (end_index - 1) and a message will be included in the response.

inputs.name: markdown_text
inputs.description: Markdown content to insert or replace in the document section. Large content is automatically split into smaller batches to avoid API limits. Also accepts 'markdown', 'content', 'text', 'body', 'markdownText', or 'markdown_content'.

requiredInputs.name: document_id
requiredInputs.description: The unique ID of the Google Docs document to update. This is the alphanumeric string found in the document URL: https://docs.google.com/document/d/{DOCUMENT_ID}/edit. Valid IDs are typically 44 characters long and contain only letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores (e.g., '1BxiMVs0XRA5nFMdKvBdBZjgmUUqptlbs74OgvE2upms'). To get a document ID: create a new document using GOOGLEDOCS_CREATE_DOCUMENT, or extract it from an existing document's URL. Also accepts parameter aliases: 'id', 'doc_id', 'documentId', or 'docId'.

requiredInputs.name: markdown_text
requiredInputs.description: Markdown content to insert or replace in the document section. Large content is automatically split into smaller batches to avoid API limits. Also accepts 'markdown', 'content', 'text', 'body', 'markdownText', or 'markdown_content'.

optionalInputs.name: end_index
optionalInputs.description: Optional one-based end index of the content to replace (exclusive); must be greater than or equal to start_index. If end_index is not provided, the content is inserted at the start_index without replacing existing content. Requires start_index to be provided - if start_index is omitted (append mode), end_index is ignored. Important: Use the exact indices from GOOGLEDOCS_GET_DOCUMENT_BY_ID without modification. The Google Docs API does not allow deleting the last newline character of a document segment (body, header, footer, etc.), so ensure end_index does not include the final segment newline.

optionalInputs.name: start_index

name: Update Document Style
description: Tool to update the overall document style, such as page size, margins, and default text direction. Use when you need to modify the global style settings of a Google Document.

inputs.name: fields
inputs.description: A comma-separated list of fields to update (using camelCase API names like 'marginTop', 'pageSize'). Use '*' to automatically update only fields that have values set in document_style. For example: "pageSize,marginTop,marginBottom". When '*' is specified, the action automatically computes the field mask from the provided document_style fields.

inputs.name: tab_id
inputs.description: The ID of the tab to update the style for. If not provided, the first tab is used.

inputs.name: document_id
inputs.description: The ID of the document to update.

inputs.name: document_style
inputs.description: The DocumentStyle object with the new style settings.

inputs.name: pageSize
inputs.description: The size of a page in the document.

inputs.name: marginTop
inputs.description: The top margin of the page.

inputs.name: background
inputs.description: The background of the document. Documents cannot have a transparent background color. Structure: {'color': {'color': {'rgbColor': {'red': float, 'green': float, 'blue': float}}}}. RGB values should be between 0.0 and 1.0.

inputs.name: marginLeft
inputs.description: The left margin of the page.

inputs.name: marginRight
inputs.description: The right margin of the page.

inputs.name: marginBottom
inputs.description: The bottom margin of the page.

inputs.name: marginFooter
inputs.description: The footer margin of the page (distance from bottom of page to footer content).

inputs.name: marginHeader

inputs.description: Document-level format settings.

inputs.name: defaultHeaderId
inputs.description: The ID of the default header. If unset, the document inherits the default header from the parent document. This property is read-only.

inputs.name: oddPageFooterId
inputs.description: The ID of the footer for odd pages. If unset, the document inherits the odd page footer from the parent document. This property is read-only.

inputs.name: oddPageHeaderId
inputs.description: The ID of the header for odd pages. If unset, the document inherits the odd page header from the parent document. This property is read-only.

inputs.name: pageNumberStart
inputs.description: The page number from which to start counting the number of pages.

inputs.name: evenPageFooterId

inputs.name: evenPageHeaderId

When '*' is specified, the action automatically computes the field mask from the provided document_style fields.

name: Update draft
description: Updates (replaces) an existing Gmail draft's content in-place by draft ID. This action replaces the entire draft content with the new message - it does not patch individual fields. All fields are optional; if not provided, you should provide complete draft content to avoid data loss.

inputs.name: cc
inputs.description: Carbon Copy (CC) recipients' email addresses. Each must be a valid email address or display name format.

inputs.name: bcc
inputs.description: Blind Carbon Copy (BCC) recipients' email addresses. Each must be a valid email address or display name format.

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: Email body content (plain text or HTML); is_html must be True if HTML. If not provided, previous body is preserved. Can also be provided as 'message_body'.

inputs.name: is_html
inputs.description:
Set to True if body is already formatted HTML. When False, plain text newlines are auto-converted to
tags.

inputs.name: subject
inputs.description: Email subject line. If not provided, previous subject is preserved.

inputs.name: user_id
inputs.description: User's email address or 'me' for the authenticated user.

inputs.name: draft_id
inputs.description: The ID of the draft to update. Must be a valid draft ID from GMAIL_LIST_DRAFTS or GMAIL_CREATE_EMAIL_DRAFT.

inputs.name: thread_id
inputs.description: ID of an existing Gmail thread. If provided, the draft will be part of this thread.

inputs.name: attachment
inputs.description: File to attach to the draft. Replaces any existing attachments.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The filename that will be used when uploading the file to the destination service

inputs.name: s3key
inputs.description: The S3 key of a publicly accessible file, typically returned from a previous download action that stored the file in S3. This key references an existing file that can be uploaded to another service.

inputs.name: mimetype
inputs.description: The MIME type of the file

inputs.name: recipient_email
inputs.description: Primary recipient's email address. Must be a valid email address (e.g., 'user@example.com') or display name format (e.g., 'John Doe <user@example.com>'). Optional - if not provided, previous recipients are preserved.

inputs.name: extra_recipients
inputs.description: Additional 'To' recipients' email addresses. Each must be a valid email address or display name format. Should only be used if recipient_email is also provided.

requiredInputs.name: draft_id
requiredInputs.description: The ID of the draft to update. Must be a valid draft ID from GMAIL_LIST_DRAFTS or GMAIL_CREATE_EMAIL_DRAFT.

optionalInputs.name: cc
optionalInputs.description: Carbon Copy (CC) recipients' email addresses. Each must be a valid email address or display name format.

optionalInputs.name: bcc
optionalInputs.description: Blind Carbon Copy (BCC) recipients' email addresses. Each must be a valid email address or display name format.

optionalInputs.name: body
optionalInputs.description: Email body content (plain text or HTML); is_html must be True if HTML. If not provided, previous body is preserved. Can also be provided as 'message_body'.

optionalInputs.name: is_html
optionalInputs.description:
Set to True if body is already formatted HTML. When False, plain text newlines are auto-converted to
tags.

optionalInputs.name: subject
optionalInputs.description: Email subject line. If not provided, previous subject is preserved.

optionalInputs.name: user_id
optionalInputs.description: User's email address or 'me' for the authenticated user.

optionalInputs.name: thread_id
optionalInputs.description: ID of an existing Gmail thread. If provided, the draft will be part of this thread.

optionalInputs.name: attachment

name: Update Shared Drive
description: Tool to update the metadata for a shared drive. Use when you need to modify properties like the name, theme, background image, or restrictions of a shared drive.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The new name for the shared drive.

inputs.name: hidden
inputs.description: Whether the shared drive is hidden from the default view.

inputs.name: driveId
inputs.description: The ID of the shared drive to update.

inputs.name: themeId
inputs.description: The ID of a theme to apply to the shared drive. Cannot be set if colorRgb or backgroundImageFile are set.

inputs.name: colorRgb
inputs.description: The color of this shared drive as an RGB hex string (e.g., "#FF0000"). Cannot be set if themeId is set.

inputs.name: restrictions
inputs.description: A set of restrictions to apply to the shared drive.

inputs.name: domainUsersOnly
inputs.description: If true, restricts access to users of the domain to which the shared drive belongs.

inputs.name: driveMembersOnly
inputs.description: If true, restricts access to items inside the shared drive to its members.

inputs.name: adminManagedRestrictions
inputs.description: If true, requires administrative privileges to modify restrictions.

inputs.name: copyRequiresWriterPermission
inputs.description: If true, disables copy, print, or download options for readers and commenters.

inputs.name: sharingFoldersRequiresOrganizerPermission
inputs.description: If true, only users with the organizer role can share folders. If false, users with either the organizer or file organizer role can share folders.

inputs.name: backgroundImageFile
inputs.description: An image file and cropping parameters for the shared drive's background. Cannot be set if themeId is set.

inputs.name: id
inputs.description: The ID of an image file in Google Drive to use for the background image.

inputs.name: width
inputs.description: The width of the cropped image (0.0 to 1.0). The height is computed (aspect ratio 80:9).

inputs.name: xCoordinate
inputs.description: The X coordinate of the upper left corner of the cropping area in the background image (0.0 to 1.0).

inputs.name: yCoordinate
inputs.description: The Y coordinate of the upper left corner of the cropping area in the background image (0.0 to 1.0).

inputs.name: useDomainAdminAccess
inputs.description: If set to true, the request is issued as a domain administrator.

requiredInputs.name: driveId
requiredInputs.description: The ID of the shared drive to update.

optionalInputs.name: name
optionalInputs.description: The new name for the shared drive.

optionalInputs.description: Whether the shared drive is hidden from the default view.

optionalInputs.description: The ID of a theme to apply to the shared drive. Cannot be set if colorRgb or backgroundImageFile are set.

name: Update Google event
description: Updates an existing event in Google Calendar. REQUIRES event_id - you MUST first search for the event using GOOGLECALENDAR_FIND_EVENT or GOOGLECALENDAR_EVENTS_LIST to obtain the event_id. This is a full PUT replacement: omitted fields (including attendees, reminders, recurrence, conferencing) are cleared. Always provide the complete desired event state. Use GOOGLECALENDAR_PATCH_EVENT instead for partial edits.

inputs.name: summary
inputs.description: Summary (title) of the event.

inputs.name: event_id
inputs.description: REQUIRED. The unique identifier of the event to update. This parameter is MANDATORY - events cannot be updated by title, date, or other criteria. You MUST first retrieve the event_id by using GOOGLECALENDAR_FIND_EVENT or GOOGLECALENDAR_EVENTS_LIST to search for the event, then use the returned 'id' field here.

inputs.name: location
inputs.description: Geographic location of the event as free-form text.

inputs.name: timezone
inputs.description: IANA timezone name from the timezone database (e.g., 'America/New_York', 'Europe/London', 'Asia/Jerusalem', 'UTC'). Required if datetime is naive. For recurring events, start and end must include a timeZone. If not provided, UTC is used. If datetime includes timezone info (Z or offset), this field is optional and defaults to UTC. IMPORTANT: Must be a valid IANA timezone identifier. Values like 'EST', 'PST', 'ISRAEL TIME', or other abbreviations are NOT valid IANA timezone names.

inputs.name: attendees
inputs.description: List of attendees. Each attendee can be either: (1) A string email address (e.g., 'user@example.com'), or (2) An object with 'email' (required), 'optional' (boolean, default false), 'displayName' (string), 'comment' (string), 'additionalGuests' (integer), and 'resource' (boolean). To mark an attendee as optional (not required), use object format: {'email': 'user@example.com', 'optional': true}. IMPORTANT: Only valid email addresses are accepted. Plain names cannot be used.

inputs.name: eventType
inputs.description: Type of the event, immutable post-creation. 'workingLocation' (REQUIRES Google Workspace Enterprise). Note: 'fromGmail' events cannot be created via API.

inputs.name: recurrence
inputs.description: List of RRULE, EXRULE, RDATE, EXDATE lines for recurring events. Supported frequencies: DAILY, WEEKLY, MONTHLY, YEARLY. For recurring events, start.timeZone and end.timeZone must be present. UNTIL values follow RFC 5545: date-only (YYYYMMDD) for all-day events, or UTC datetime with Z suffix (YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ) for timed events. UNTIL values with time but missing Z suffix are auto-corrected. Provide an empty list to remove recurrence so the event becomes non-recurring.

inputs.name: visibility
inputs.description: Event visibility: 'default', 'public', 'private', or 'confidential'.

inputs.name: calendar_id
inputs.description: Identifier of the Google Calendar where the event resides. The value 'primary' targets the user's primary calendar.

inputs.name: description
inputs.description: Description of the event. Can contain HTML. Optional. Must be omitted for 'birthday' event type.

inputs.name: end_datetime
inputs.description: Event end time in ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS. When provided, this parameter takes precedence over event_duration_hour and event_duration_minutes. If not provided, the end time is calculated using start_datetime + duration. Must be after start_datetime. Fractional seconds and timezone info will be automatically stripped if provided. Examples: '2025-01-16T14:30:00', '2025-01-16T14:30'.

inputs.name: send_updates
inputs.description: Options for who should receive notifications about event changes.

inputs.name: transparency
inputs.description: 'opaque' (busy) or 'transparent' (available).

inputs.name: start_datetime
inputs.description: REQUIRED. Event start time in ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS. IMPORTANT: Natural language expressions like 'tomorrow', 'next Monday', '2pm tomorrow' are NOT supported and will be rejected. You must provide the exact date and time in ISO format. Fractional seconds (e.g., .000) and timezone info (Z, +, -) will be automatically stripped if provided. Examples: '2025-01-16T13:00:00', '2025-01-16T13:00'.

inputs.name: guestsCanModify
inputs.description: If True, guests can modify the event.

inputs.name: birthdayProperties
inputs.description: Properties for birthday events.

inputs.name: type
inputs.description: Type of birthday event: 'birthday', 'anniversary', or 'other'. Defaults to 'birthday'.

inputs.name: contact
inputs.description: Contact ID in format 'people/c12345' from Google People API. REQUIRED when type is 'anniversary' or 'other'. MUST BE OMITTED when type is 'birthday' (the API forbids contact field for type='birthday').

inputs.name: customTypeName
inputs.description: Custom type name when type is 'other'. Requires valid contact field.

inputs.name: create_meeting_room
inputs.description: Defaults to True. When True, for CREATE operations creates a Google Meet link; for UPDATE operations preserves existing conference data if present, or adds a new Meet link if none exists. Google Workspace accounts will successfully receive a Meet link. Personal Gmail accounts and other unsupported accounts will gracefully fallback to creating an event without a Meet link when conference creation fails. Set to False to skip Meet link operations (won't create new or modify existing conference data). The fallback ensures event creation succeeds even when conference features are unavailable due to account limitations.

inputs.name: event_duration_hour
inputs.description: Number of hours for the event duration. Supports multi-day events (e.g., 240 hours = 10 days). For durations under 1 hour, use event_duration_minutes instead. Ignored if end_datetime is provided.

inputs.name: extended_properties
inputs.description: Extended properties of the event for storing custom metadata. Contains 'private' (visible only on this calendar) and/or 'shared' (visible to all attendees) dictionaries mapping string keys to string values. Example: {'private': {'key1': 'value1'}, 'shared': {'key2': 'value2'}}

inputs.name: focusTimeProperties
inputs.description: Properties for focusTime events. REQUIRES Google Workspace Enterprise account with Focus Time feature enabled.

inputs.name: chatStatus
inputs.description: Chat status during focus time: 'active' or 'doNotDisturb'.

inputs.name: declineMessage
inputs.description: Message to include in declined meeting invitations. Only used when autoDeclineMode is set.

inputs.name: autoDeclineMode
inputs.description: Auto decline mode: 'declineNone' (no invitations declined), 'declineAllConflictingInvitations' (all conflicting invitations declined), or 'declineOnlyNewConflictingInvitations' (only new conflicting invitations declined).

inputs.name: guestsCanInviteOthers
inputs.description: Whether attendees other than the organizer can invite others to the event.

inputs.name: outOfOfficeProperties
inputs.description: Properties for outOfOffice events.

inputs.name: declineMessage
inputs.description: Message to include in declined meeting invitations. Only used when autoDeclineMode is set.

inputs.name: autoDeclineMode
inputs.description: Auto decline mode: 'declineNone' (no invitations declined), 'declineAllConflictingInvitations' (all conflicting invitations declined), or 'declineOnlyNewConflictingInvitations' (only new conflicting invitations declined). RECURRING EVENT RESTRICTION: For recurring out-of-office events, ONLY 'declineOnlyNewConflictingInvitations' is allowed. Cannot use 'declineAllConflictingInvitations' for recurring OOO (Google Calendar prevents retroactively declining existing meetings). REQUIRES Google Workspace (paid business subscription, e.g., Business Starter at $6/user/month). Personal Gmail accounts cannot use this feature.

inputs.name: event_duration_minutes
inputs.description: Duration in minutes (0-59 ONLY). NEVER use 60+ minutes - use event_duration_hour=1 instead. Maximum value is 59. Combined duration (hours + minutes) must be greater than 0. Ignored if end_datetime is provided.

inputs.name: guestsCanSeeOtherGuests
inputs.description: Whether attendees other than the organizer can see who the event's attendees are.

inputs.name: workingLocationProperties
inputs.description:
Properties for workingLocation events. REQUIRES Google Workspace Enterprise.

Constraints discovered from testing:
- Must set transparency='transparent' and visibility='public'
- Description must be omitted
- Depending on 'type', include one of 'homeOffice', 'officeLocation', or 'customLocation'

inputs.description: Type of working location ('homeOffice' | 'officeLocation' | 'customLocation').

inputs.name: homeOffice
inputs.description:
Empty object marker for home office working location.

This is used to indicate the user is working from home.
Google Calendar API accepts an empty object for this field.

inputs.name: customLocation
inputs.description: Custom working location with a display label.

inputs.name: label
inputs.description: Label for a custom working location (e.g., 'Client site').

inputs.name: officeLocation
inputs.description: Office-based working location details.

inputs.name: label
inputs.description: Office name displayed in Calendar clients (e.g., building name).

inputs.name: deskId
inputs.description: Optional desk identifier.

inputs.name: floorId
inputs.description: Optional floor identifier.

inputs.description: Optional building identifier from org Resources.

inputs.name: floorSectionId
inputs.description: Optional floor section identifier.

name: Update existing document
description: Applies programmatic edits, such as text insertion, deletion, or formatting, to a specified Google Doc using the `batchUpdate` API method.

inputs.name: edit_docs
inputs.description:
(Required) A list of requests to execute on the document. Each request must be a dict containing exactly ONE of the valid request types listed below.

Common request types:
replaceAllText, insertText, updateTextStyle, createParagraphBullets, deleteParagraphBullets, createNamedRange, deleteNamedRange, updateParagraphStyle, deleteContentRange, insertInlineImage, insertTable, insertTableRow, insertTableColumn, deleteTableRow, deleteTableColumn, insertPageBreak, deletePositionedObject, updateTableColumnProperties, updateTableCellStyle, updateTableRowStyle, replaceImage, updateDocumentStyle, mergeTableCells, unmergeTableCells, createHeader, createFooter, createFootnote, replaceNamedRangeContent, updateSectionStyle, insertSectionBreak, deleteHeader, deleteFooter, pinTableHeaderRows, addDocumentTab, deleteTab, updateDocumentTabProperties, insertPerson.

Do NOT use request types from other Google APIs (Slides, Sheets, etc.).
For full request schemas, see https://developers.google.com/docs/api/reference/rest/v1/documents/batchUpdate.

SEGMENT IDs: Use empty string "" for document body, or a header/footer/footnote ID from the document. Internal 'kix.' IDs are NOT valid segment IDs and will cause "Segment with ID ... was not found" errors. Call GOOGLEDOCS_GET_DOCUMENT_BY_ID to get valid IDs.

TAB IDs: When omitted, operations target the first tab. Invalid tab IDs cause errors. Get valid tab IDs from GOOGLEDOCS_GET_DOCUMENT_BY_ID at tabs[].tabProperties.tabId.

BATCH INSERT OPERATIONS: Operations execute sequentially. Each insertText shifts subsequent indices. For multiple inserts at specific positions, process in REVERSE index order (highest first). For appending, use endOfSegmentLocation for each insert. Auto-correction: insertText without location/endOfSegmentLocation automatically appends to document body.

INDEX BOUNDARIES: location.index must be LESS than the segment end index (valid: 1 to N-1). To insert at the end, use endOfSegmentLocation instead.

PARAGRAPH BOUNDARIES: Text can only be inserted inside existing paragraphs, not at structural boundaries (e.g., a table's start index). For table cells, add +1 or +2 to the cell's startIndex.

deleteContentRange: The range CANNOT include the trailing newline at segment end. If segment ends at N, use endIndex: N-1.

deleteTableRow: Requires tableCellLocation object (NOT top-level rowIndex/tableStartLocation). Structure: { tableCellLocation: { tableStartLocation: { index: N }, rowIndex: R, columnIndex: C } }.

updateTableCellStyle: Use EITHER tableStartLocation (all cells) OR tableRange (specific cells), not both.

Style updates (updateParagraphStyle, updateTextStyle, updateTableCellStyle, updateDocumentStyle): The 'fields' parameter is auto-populated from style keys if omitted.

Document title CANNOT be updated via batchUpdate - use Google Drive API instead.

updateDocumentStyle valid fields: background, marginTop, marginBottom, marginLeft, marginRight, marginHeader, marginFooter, pageSize, pageNumberStart, useEvenPageHeaderFooter, useFirstPageHeaderFooter, flipPageOrientation (NOT "title").

inputs.name: document_id
inputs.description: (Required) The unique identifier of the Google Docs document to be updated.

requiredInputs.name: edit_docs
requiredInputs.description:
(Required) A list of requests to execute on the document. Each request must be a dict containing exactly ONE of the valid request types listed below.

Common request types:
replaceAllText, insertText, updateTextStyle, createParagraphBullets, deleteParagraphBullets, createNamedRange, deleteNamedRange, updateParagraphStyle, deleteContentRange, insertInlineImage, insertTable, insertTableRow, insertTableColumn, deleteTableRow, deleteTableColumn, insertPageBreak, deletePositionedObject, updateTableColumnProperties, updateTableCellStyle, updateTableRowStyle, replaceImage, updateDocumentStyle, mergeTableCells, unmergeTableCells, createHeader, createFooter, createFootnote, replaceNamedRangeContent, updateSectionStyle, insertSectionBreak, deleteHeader, deleteFooter, pinTableHeaderRows, addDocumentTab, deleteTab, updateDocumentTabProperties, insertPerson.

Do NOT use request types from other Google APIs (Slides, Sheets, etc.).
For full request schemas, see https://developers.google.com/docs/api/reference/rest/v1/documents/batchUpdate.

SEGMENT IDs: Use empty string "" for document body, or a header/footer/footnote ID from the document. Internal 'kix.' IDs are NOT valid segment IDs and will cause "Segment with ID ... was not found" errors. Call GOOGLEDOCS_GET_DOCUMENT_BY_ID to get valid IDs.

TAB IDs: When omitted, operations target the first tab. Invalid tab IDs cause errors. Get valid tab IDs from GOOGLEDOCS_GET_DOCUMENT_BY_ID at tabs[].tabProperties.tabId.

BATCH INSERT OPERATIONS: Operations execute sequentially. Each insertText shifts subsequent indices. For multiple inserts at specific positions, process in REVERSE index order (highest first). For appending, use endOfSegmentLocation for each insert. Auto-correction: insertText without location/endOfSegmentLocation automatically appends to document body.

INDEX BOUNDARIES: location.index must be LESS than the segment end index (valid: 1 to N-1). To insert at the end, use endOfSegmentLocation instead.

PARAGRAPH BOUNDARIES: Text can only be inserted inside existing paragraphs, not at structural boundaries (e.g., a table's start index). For table cells, add +1 or +2 to the cell's startIndex.

deleteContentRange: The range CANNOT include the trailing newline at segment end. If segment ends at N, use endIndex: N-1.

deleteTableRow: Requires tableCellLocation object (NOT top-level rowIndex/tableStartLocation). Structure: { tableCellLocation: { tableStartLocation: { index: N }, rowIndex: R, columnIndex: C } }.

updateTableCellStyle: Use EITHER tableStartLocation (all cells) OR tableRange (specific cells), not both.

Style updates (updateParagraphStyle, updateTextStyle, updateTableCellStyle, updateDocumentStyle): The 'fields' parameter is auto-populated from style keys if omitted.

Document title CANNOT be updated via batchUpdate - use Google Drive API instead.

updateDocumentStyle valid fields: background, marginTop, marginBottom, marginLeft, marginRight, marginHeader, marginFooter, pageSize, pageNumberStart, useEvenPageHeaderFooter, useFirstPageHeaderFooter, flipPageOrientation (NOT "title").

name: Update File Metadata (PATCH v2)
description: Tool to update file metadata using the Drive API v2 PATCH method. Use when you need to modify file properties like title, description, or labels using patch semantics.

inputs.name: ocr
inputs.description: Whether to attempt OCR on .jpg, .png, .gif, or .pdf uploads.

inputs.name: title
inputs.description: The title of the file. Used to change the name of the file.

inputs.name: fileId
inputs.description: The ID of the file to update.

inputs.name: labels
inputs.description: A group of labels for the file. For example: {'starred': true, 'trashed': false, 'restricted': false, 'viewed': true}.

inputs.name: pinned
inputs.description: Whether to pin the new revision. A file can have a maximum of 200 pinned revisions.

inputs.name: mimeType
inputs.description: The MIME type of the file.

inputs.name: addParents
inputs.description: Comma-separated list of parent IDs to add.

inputs.name: properties
inputs.description: The list of properties.

inputs.name: description
inputs.description: A short description of the file.

inputs.name: newRevision
inputs.description: Whether a blob upload should create a new revision. If not set, a new revision is created.

inputs.name: ocrLanguage
inputs.description: If ocr is true, hints at the language to use. Valid values are BCP 47 codes.

inputs.name: modifiedDate
inputs.description: Last time this file was modified by anyone (RFC 3339 date-time). Requires setModifiedDate=true.

inputs.name: includeLabels
inputs.description: A comma-separated list of IDs of labels to include in the labelInfo part of the response.

inputs.name: indexableText
inputs.description: Indexable text attributes for the file (can be used to improve fulltext queries).

inputs.name: removeParents
inputs.description: Comma-separated list of parent IDs to remove.

inputs.name: setModifiedDate
inputs.description: Whether to set the modified date using the value supplied in the request body.

inputs.name: writersCanShare
inputs.description: Whether writers can share the document with other users.

inputs.name: updateViewedDate
inputs.description: Whether to update the view date after successfully updating the file.

inputs.name: supportsAllDrives
inputs.description: Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives. Defaults to true.

inputs.name: timedTextLanguage
inputs.description: The language of the timed text.

inputs.name: timedTextTrackName
inputs.description: The timed text track name.

inputs.name: includePermissionsForView
inputs.description: Specifies which additional view's permissions to include in the response. Only 'published' is supported.

inputs.name: useContentAsIndexableText
inputs.description: Whether to use the content as indexable text.

name: Update File (Metadata)
description: Updates file metadata. Uses PATCH semantics (partial update) as per Google Drive API v3 — only explicitly provided fields are updated, so omit fields you do not intend to overwrite. Use this tool to modify attributes of an existing file like its name, description, or parent folders. To move a file, supply add_parents and remove_parents together; omitting remove_parents creates multiple parents, omitting add_parents can orphan the file. Bulk updates may trigger 429 Too Many Requests; apply exponential backoff. Note: supports metadata updates only; file content updates are not yet implemented.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The name of the file. Google Drive does not enforce name uniqueness within a folder; duplicate names are allowed and can cause ambiguous results when searching by name.

inputs.name: fileId
inputs.description: The ID of the file to update.

inputs.name: starred
inputs.description: Whether the user has starred the file.

inputs.name: mime_type
inputs.description: The MIME type of the file. Google Drive will attempt to automatically detect an appropriate value from uploaded content if no value is provided. The value cannot be changed unless a new revision is uploaded.

inputs.name: add_parents
inputs.description: Comma-separated list of folder IDs (not folder names) to add as parents. Folder IDs are alphanumeric strings typically 20+ characters long (e.g., '1A2B3C4D5E6F7G8H9I0J'). Folder names will not work and will cause a 'Parent folder not found' error. Moving a file requires pairing with remove_parents (source folder ID); omitting remove_parents results in multiple parents. Reparenting to a shared folder changes collaborator access to that folder's permissions.

inputs.name: description
inputs.description: A short description of the file.

inputs.name: ocr_language
inputs.description: A language hint for OCR processing during image import (ISO 639-1 code).

inputs.name: remove_parents
inputs.description: Comma-separated list of folder IDs (not folder names) to remove as parents. Folder IDs are alphanumeric strings typically 20+ characters long (e.g., '1A2B3C4D5E6F7G8H9I0J'). Folder names will not work and will cause a 'Parent folder not found' error.

inputs.name: writers_can_share
inputs.description: Whether writers can share the document with other users.

inputs.name: supports_all_drives
inputs.description: Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives. Defaults to true to ensure compatibility with shared drive files.

inputs.name: keep_revision_forever
inputs.description: Whether to set this revision of the file to be kept forever. This is only applicable to files with binary content in Google Drive. Only 200 revisions for the file can be kept forever. If the limit is reached, try deleting pinned revisions.

inputs.name: use_domain_admin_access
inputs.description: Whether the requesting application is using domain-wide delegation to access content belonging to a user in a different domain. This is only applicable to files with binary content in Google Drive.

inputs.name: viewers_can_copy_content
inputs.description: Whether viewers are prevented from copying content of the file.

requiredInputs.name: fileId
requiredInputs.description: The ID of the file to update.

optionalInputs.name: name
optionalInputs.description: The name of the file. Google Drive does not enforce name uniqueness within a folder; duplicate names are allowed and can cause ambiguous results when searching by name.

optionalInputs.name: starred
optionalInputs.description: Whether the user has starred the file.

optionalInputs.description: The MIME type of the file. Google Drive will attempt to automatically detect an appropriate value from uploaded content if no value is provided. The value cannot be changed unless a new revision is uploaded.

Folder names will not work and will cause a 'Parent folder not found' error. Moving a file requires pairing with remove_parents (source folder ID); omitting remove_parents results in multiple parents. Reparenting to a shared folder changes collaborator access to that folder's permissions.

name: Update File Revision Metadata
description: Updates ONLY the metadata properties of a specific file revision (keepForever, published, publishAuto, publishedOutsideDomain). IMPORTANT: This action does NOT update file content. To update file content, use EDIT_FILE or UPDATE_FILE_PUT instead. This action requires BOTH file_id AND revision_id parameters. Use LIST_REVISIONS to get available revision IDs for a file. Valid parameters: file_id (required), revision_id (required), keep_forever, published, publish_auto, published_outside_domain. Invalid parameters (use other actions): file_contents, mime_type, content, name - these are NOT supported by this action.

inputs.name: file_id
inputs.description: Required. The ID of the file whose revision metadata you want to update. Use LIST_FILES or FIND_FILE to get the file ID.

inputs.name: published
inputs.description: Whether this revision is published. This is only applicable to Docs Editors files.

inputs.name: keepForever
inputs.description: Whether to keep this revision forever, even if it is no longer the head revision. If not set, the revision will be automatically purged 30 days after newer content is uploaded. This can be set on a maximum of 200 revisions for a file. This field is only applicable to files with binary content in Drive.

inputs.name: publishAuto
inputs.description: Whether subsequent revisions will be automatically republished. This is only applicable to Docs Editors files.

inputs.name: revision_id
inputs.description: Required. The ID of the revision to update. Use LIST_REVISIONS to get available revision IDs for a file.

inputs.name: publishedOutsideDomain
inputs.description: Whether this revision is published outside the domain. This is only applicable to Docs Editors files.

name: Update IMAP settings
description: Tool to update IMAP settings for a Gmail account. Use when you need to modify IMAP configuration such as enabling/disabling IMAP, setting auto-expunge behavior, or configuring folder size limits.

inputs.name: enabled
inputs.description: Whether IMAP is enabled for the account.

inputs.name: user_id
inputs.description: The user's email address. The special value 'me' can be used to indicate the authenticated user.

inputs.name: autoExpunge
inputs.description: If this value is true, Gmail will immediately expunge a message when it is marked as deleted in IMAP. Otherwise, Gmail will wait for an update from the client before expunging messages marked as deleted.

inputs.name: maxFolderSize
inputs.description: An optional limit on the number of messages that an IMAP folder may contain. Legal values are 0, 1000, 2000, 5000 or 10000. A value of zero is interpreted to mean that there is no limit.

inputs.name: expungeBehavior
inputs.description: The action that will be executed on a message when it is marked as deleted and expunged from the last visible IMAP folder. Possible values: 'expungeBehaviorUnspecified' (Unspecified behavior), 'archive' (Archive messages marked as deleted), 'trash' (Move messages marked as deleted to the trash), 'deleteForever' (Immediately and permanently delete messages marked as deleted).

optionalInputs.name: enabled
optionalInputs.description: Whether IMAP is enabled for the account.

optionalInputs.name: user_id
optionalInputs.description: The user's email address. The special value 'me' can be used to indicate the authenticated user.

optionalInputs.name: autoExpunge

Possible values: 'expungeBehaviorUnspecified' (Unspecified behavior), 'archive' (Archive messages marked as deleted), 'trash' (Move messages marked as deleted to the trash), 'deleteForever' (Immediately and permanently delete messages marked as deleted).

name: Update Language Settings
description: Tool to update the language settings for a Gmail user. Use when you need to change the display language preference for the authenticated user or a specific Gmail account. The returned displayLanguage may differ from the requested value if Gmail selects a close variant.

inputs.name: user_id
inputs.description: The email address of the Gmail user whose language settings are to be updated, or the special value 'me' to indicate the currently authenticated user.

inputs.name: display_language
inputs.description: The language to display Gmail in, formatted as an RFC 3066 Language Tag (e.g., 'en-GB' for British English, 'fr' for French, 'ja' for Japanese, 'es' for Spanish, 'de' for German, 'en' for English). The set of languages supported by Gmail evolves over time. Note: Gmail may save a close variant if the requested language is not directly supported. For example, if you request a regional variant that's not available, Gmail may save the base language instead.

requiredInputs.name: display_language

The set of languages supported by Gmail evolves over time. Note: Gmail may save a close variant if the requested language is not directly supported. For example, if you request a regional variant that's not available, Gmail may save the base language instead.

optionalInputs.name: user_id

name: Update Permission
description: Tool to update a permission with patch semantics. Use when you need to modify an existing permission for a file or shared drive. Inherited or domain-managed permissions may not be editable; verify editability with GOOGLEDRIVE_LIST_PERMISSIONS before updating.

inputs.name: fileId
inputs.description: The ID of the file or shared drive.

inputs.name: permission
inputs.description: The permission resource to update. Only 'role' and 'expirationTime' can be updated. Role changes take effect immediately and can be difficult to reverse; confirm intent before applying.

inputs.name: role
inputs.description: Permission roles that can be granted in Google Drive.

inputs.name: expirationTime
inputs.description: The time at which this permission will expire (RFC 3339 date-time). Only settable on 'user' and 'group' permissions; the Google Drive API rejects expirationTime on 'anyone' (e.g., permissionId 'anyone'/'anyoneWithLink') and 'domain' type permissions with a 403 error.

inputs.name: permissionId
inputs.description: The ID of the permission. For anyone-type permissions, use 'anyone' as the permission ID.

inputs.name: removeExpiration
inputs.description: Whether to remove the expiration date.

inputs.name: supportsAllDrives
inputs.description: Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives. Must be set to true when operating on shared drives; omitting this causes the request to fail.

inputs.name: transferOwnership
inputs.description: Whether to transfer ownership to the specified user and downgrade the current owner to a writer. This parameter is required as an acknowledgement of the side effect when set to true.

inputs.name: useDomainAdminAccess
inputs.description: Issue the request as a domain administrator; if set to true, then the requester will be granted access if the file ID parameter refers to a shared drive and the requester is an administrator of the domain to which the shared drive belongs.

inputs.name: enforceExpansiveAccess
inputs.description: Whether the request should enforce expansive access rules. This field is deprecated, it is recommended to use `permissionDetails` instead.

requiredInputs.name: fileId
requiredInputs.description: The ID of the file or shared drive.

requiredInputs.description: The permission resource to update. Only 'role' and 'expirationTime' can be updated. Role changes take effect immediately and can be difficult to reverse; confirm intent before applying.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: Required. The resource name of the Property to update. Must be in the exact format: properties/{property_id} where property_id is a numeric identifier. IMPORTANT: Must start with 'properties/' prefix, contain no forward slashes in the property_id, have no trailing slashes, and no additional path segments. Valid example: 'properties/489591273'. Invalid examples: '489591273' (missing prefix), 'properties/489591273/' (trailing slash), 'properties/489591273/extra' (extra segments)

inputs.name: parent
inputs.description: Immutable. Resource name of this property's logical parent. Format: accounts/{account}, properties/{property}. Example: 'accounts/100', 'properties/101'

inputs.name: timeZone
inputs.description: Required. Reporting Time Zone, used as the day boundary for reports. Format: IANA time zone. Example: 'America/Los_Angeles'

inputs.name: updateMask
inputs.description: Required. The list of fields to be updated. Field names must be in snake case (e.g., 'display_name', 'time_zone', 'currency_code'). Omitted fields will not be updated. To replace the entire entity, use one path with the string '*' to match all fields. Common fields: 'display_name', 'time_zone', 'currency_code', 'industry_category'

inputs.name: displayName
inputs.description: Human-readable display name for this property. The max allowed display name length is 100 UTF-16 code units.

inputs.name: currencyCode
inputs.description: The currency type used in reports involving monetary values. Format: ISO 4217. Examples: 'USD', 'EUR', 'JPY'

inputs.name: propertyType
inputs.description: Property type for a property.

inputs.name: industryCategory
inputs.description: Industry category for a property.

name: Update send-as alias
description: Tool to update a send-as alias for a Gmail user. Use when you need to modify display name, signature, reply-to address, or SMTP settings for a send-as email address. Gmail sanitizes HTML signatures before saving. Addresses other than the primary can only be updated by service accounts with domain-wide authority.

inputs.name: user_id
inputs.description: The email address of the Gmail user whose send-as alias to update, or the special value 'me' to indicate the authenticated user.

inputs.name: smtp_msa
inputs.description: SMTP relay configuration for the send-as alias.

inputs.name: host
inputs.description: The hostname of the SMTP service. Required when configuring SMTP.

inputs.name: port
inputs.description: The port of the SMTP service. Required when configuring SMTP.

inputs.name: password
inputs.description: SMTP authentication password. Write-only field, never appears in responses.

inputs.name: username
inputs.description: SMTP authentication username. Write-only field, never appears in responses.

inputs.name: securityMode
inputs.description: Protocol for securing SMTP communication. Required when configuring SMTP.

inputs.name: signature
inputs.description: Optional HTML signature for messages composed with this alias in Gmail web UI. Gmail sanitizes HTML before saving. Only added to new emails.

inputs.name: is_default
inputs.description: Set to true to make this the default 'From:' address for composing messages and vacation auto-replies. Setting true makes the previous default false. Only legal writable value is true.

inputs.name: display_name
inputs.description: Name to appear in 'From:' header. For custom from addresses, Gmail populates with primary account name if empty. Admin restrictions may silently fail updates to primary login name.

inputs.name: send_as_email
inputs.description: The send-as alias email address to update. This is the email address that appears in the 'From:' header.

inputs.name: treat_as_alias
inputs.description: Whether Gmail treats this address as an alias for the user's primary email. Only applies to custom from aliases.

inputs.name: reply_to_address
inputs.description: Optional email address for 'Reply-To:' header. Gmail omits header if empty.

requiredInputs.name: send_as_email
requiredInputs.description: The send-as alias email address to update. This is the email address that appears in the 'From:' header.

name: Update Sheet Properties
description: Tool to update properties of a sheet (worksheet) within a Google Spreadsheet, such as its title, index, visibility, tab color, or grid properties. Use this when you need to modify the metadata or appearance of a specific sheet.

inputs.name: spreadsheetId
inputs.description: The ID of the spreadsheet containing the sheet to update.

inputs.name: responseRanges
inputs.description: Limits the ranges included in the response spreadsheet. Meaningful only if includeSpreadsheetInResponse is true. Ranges should be in A1 notation (e.g., 'Sheet1!A1:B2').

inputs.name: updateSheetProperties
inputs.description: The details of the sheet properties to update.

inputs.name: fields
inputs.description: A comma-separated string specifying which properties to update. Uses FieldMask format. For example, to update the title and index, use "title,index". To update all mutable sheet properties, use "*". If not provided, fields will be inferred from the properties being updated.

inputs.name: properties
inputs.description: The properties to update.

inputs.name: index
inputs.description: The new zero-based index of the sheet.

inputs.name: title
inputs.description: The new title of the sheet.

inputs.name: hidden
inputs.description: Whether the sheet should be hidden (true) or visible (false).

inputs.name: sheetId
inputs.description: The ID of the sheet to update.

inputs.name: rightToLeft
inputs.description: Toggles the sheet's layout direction (RTL vs LTR). Note: in practice, updates may only reliably switch RTL → LTR (disable RTL). To enable RTL, create a new sheet with rightToLeft=true (GOOGLESHEETS_ADD_SHEET) and move/copy data into it.

inputs.name: tabColorStyle
inputs.description: The new tab color for the sheet.

inputs.name: rgbColor
inputs.description: Represents a color using RGB values. Specify either rgbColor or themeColor, not both.

inputs.name: red
inputs.description: The red component of the color, between 0.0 and 1.0.

inputs.name: blue
inputs.description: The blue component of the color, between 0.0 and 1.0.

inputs.name: alpha
inputs.description: The alpha component of the color, between 0.0 and 1.0.

inputs.name: green
inputs.description: The green component of the color, between 0.0 and 1.0.

inputs.name: themeColor
inputs.description: Theme color types available in Google Sheets.

inputs.name: gridProperties
inputs.description: Properties of a grid sheet.

inputs.name: rowCount
inputs.description: The number of rows in the sheet. Must be at least 1.

inputs.name: columnCount
inputs.description: The number of columns in the sheet. Must be at least 1.

inputs.name: hideGridlines
inputs.description: True if gridlines are hidden.

inputs.name: frozenRowCount
inputs.description: The number of rows to freeze at the top of the sheet. Must be less than the total number of rows in the sheet (frozenRowCount < rowCount). Setting this value equal to or greater than the total row count will cause an API error.

inputs.name: frozenColumnCount
inputs.description: The number of columns to freeze on the left side of the sheet. Must be less than the total number of columns in the sheet (frozenColumnCount < columnCount). Setting this value equal to or greater than the total column count will cause an API error.

inputs.name: rowGroupControlAfter
inputs.description: Whether the row group control toggle appears after the group (true) or before the group (false).

inputs.name: columnGroupControlAfter
inputs.description: Whether the column group control toggle appears after the group (true) or before the group (false).

inputs.name: responseIncludeGridData
inputs.description: True if grid data should be returned. Meaningful only if includeSpreadsheetInResponse is true. When true, the response will include cell data for the specified ranges.

inputs.name: includeSpreadsheetInResponse
inputs.description: Determines if the update response should include the spreadsheet resource. When true, the response will include the full updated spreadsheet.

requiredInputs.name: spreadsheetId
requiredInputs.description: The ID of the spreadsheet containing the sheet to update.

requiredInputs.name: updateSheetProperties
requiredInputs.description: The details of the sheet properties to update.

requiredInputs.name: properties
requiredInputs.description: The properties to update.

requiredInputs.name: sheetId
requiredInputs.description: The ID of the sheet to update.

optionalInputs.description: Limits the ranges included in the response spreadsheet. Meaningful only if includeSpreadsheetInResponse is true. Ranges should be in A1 notation (e.g., 'Sheet1!A1:B2').

optionalInputs.description: A comma-separated string specifying which properties to update. Uses FieldMask format. For example, to update the title and index, use "title,index". To update all mutable sheet properties, use "*". If not provided, fields will be inferred from the properties being updated.

optionalInputs.description: The new zero-based index of the sheet.

name: Update Google Meet Space
description: Updates the settings of an existing Google Meet space. Requires organizer/host privileges and the meetings.space.created OAuth scope. REQUIRED PARAMETER: - name: The space identifier (e.g., 'spaces/jQCFfuBOdN5z'). This is always required to identify which space to update. OPTIONAL PARAMETERS: - config: The new configuration settings to apply (accessType, entryPointAccess, moderation, etc.) - updateMask: Specify which fields to update. If omitted, all provided config fields are updated. Example: To change access type, provide name='spaces/abc123' and config={'accessType': 'OPEN'}

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: REQUIRED. The unique identifier of the Google Meet space to update. Format: 'spaces/{space}' where {space} is the space ID.

inputs.name: config
inputs.description: Configuration settings to update for the meeting space. Provide the fields you want to modify (e.g., accessType, entryPointAccess, moderation settings). Some settings (moderation modes, attendanceReportGenerationType, artifact generation) require specific Google Workspace editions and admin policies; unsupported settings may apply silently with no effect. Certain combinations of accessType, entryPointAccess, or moderation values may be rejected based on domain or account policies.

inputs.name: accessType
inputs.description: Access type of the meeting space.

inputs.name: moderation
inputs.description: The pre-configured moderation mode for the Meeting.

inputs.name: artifactConfig
inputs.description: Configuration pertaining to the auto-generated artifacts.

inputs.name: recordingConfig
inputs.description: Configuration for recording.

inputs.name: autoRecordingGeneration
inputs.description: Defines whether a meeting space is automatically recorded.

inputs.name: smartNotesConfig
inputs.description: Configuration for auto-smart-notes. Note: This object only contains recording, transcription, and smart notes configs - NOT attendance report settings.

inputs.name: autoSmartNotesGeneration
inputs.description: Defines whether to automatically generate a summary and recap of the meeting.

inputs.name: transcriptionConfig
inputs.description: Configuration for auto-transcript.

inputs.name: autoTranscriptionGeneration
inputs.description: Defines whether the content of a meeting is automatically transcribed.

inputs.name: entryPointAccess
inputs.description: Defines the entry points that can be used to join meetings.

inputs.name: moderationRestrictions
inputs.description: When moderation.ON, these restrictions go into effect.

inputs.name: chatRestriction
inputs.description: Defines who has permission to send chat messages.

inputs.name: presentRestriction
inputs.description: Defines who has permission to share their screen.

inputs.name: reactionRestriction
inputs.description: Defines who has permission to send reactions.

inputs.name: defaultJoinAsViewerType
inputs.description: Defines whether to restrict the default role assigned to users as viewer.

inputs.name: attendanceReportGenerationType
inputs.description: Whether attendance report is enabled for the meeting space. This is a top-level config field, NOT part of artifactConfig.

inputs.name: updateMask
inputs.description: Field mask specifying which fields to update. Comma-separated list of fully qualified field names (e.g., "config.accessType,config.entryPointAccess"). If not provided, only the fields you explicitly set in config will be updated. WARNING: Using "*" will attempt to update ALL config fields, which may cause errors if required fields like attendanceReportGenerationType are not provided. Recommend specifying only the fields being updated.

requiredInputs.name: name
requiredInputs.description: REQUIRED. The unique identifier of the Google Meet space to update. Format: 'spaces/{space}' where {space} is the space ID.

optionalInputs.name: config
optionalInputs.description: Configuration settings to update for the meeting space. Provide the fields you want to modify (e.g., accessType, entryPointAccess, moderation settings). Some settings (moderation modes, attendanceReportGenerationType, artifact generation) require specific Google Workspace editions and admin policies; unsupported settings may apply silently with no effect. Certain combinations of accessType, entryPointAccess, or moderation values may be rejected based on domain or account policies.

optionalInputs.name: accessType
optionalInputs.description: Access type of the meeting space.

optionalInputs.name: moderation
optionalInputs.description: The pre-configured moderation mode for the Meeting.

optionalInputs.name: artifactConfig
optionalInputs.description: Configuration pertaining to the auto-generated artifacts.

optionalInputs.name: recordingConfig
optionalInputs.description: Configuration for recording.

optionalInputs.name: autoRecordingGeneration
optionalInputs.description: Defines whether a meeting space is automatically recorded.

optionalInputs.name: smartNotesConfig
optionalInputs.description: Configuration for auto-smart-notes. Note: This object only contains recording, transcription, and smart notes configs - NOT attendance report settings.

optionalInputs.name: autoSmartNotesGeneration
optionalInputs.description: Defines whether to automatically generate a summary and recap of the meeting.

optionalInputs.name: transcriptionConfig
optionalInputs.description: Configuration for auto-transcript.

optionalInputs.description: Defines whether the content of a meeting is automatically transcribed.

optionalInputs.description: When moderation.ON, these restrictions go into effect.

optionalInputs.description: Defines who has permission to send chat messages.

optionalInputs.description: Defines who has permission to share their screen.

optionalInputs.description: Defines who has permission to send reactions.

If not provided, only the fields you explicitly set in config will be updated. WARNING: Using "*" will attempt to update ALL config fields, which may cause errors if required fields like attendanceReportGenerationType are not provided. Recommend specifying only the fields being updated.

name: Update Spreadsheet Properties
description: Tool to update SPREADSHEET-LEVEL properties such as the spreadsheet's title, locale, time zone, or auto-recalculation settings. Use when you need to modify the overall configuration of a Google Spreadsheet. NOTE: To update individual SHEET properties (like renaming a specific sheet/tab), use GOOGLESHEETS_UPDATE_SHEET_PROPERTIES instead.

inputs.name: fields
inputs.description: Field mask specifying which properties to update (comma-separated for multiple fields). Supports nested paths using dot notation (e.g., 'iterativeCalculationSettings.maxIterations') per Protocol Buffers FieldMask specification. The root 'properties' is implied and must not be included. Special case: When updating 'spreadsheetTheme', use the field mask 'spreadsheetTheme' (not nested paths like 'spreadsheetTheme.primaryFontFamily') and provide the complete theme object with all required fields. Wildcard '*' updates all properties.

inputs.name: properties
inputs.description: The spreadsheet-level properties to update (e.g., title, locale, timeZone, autoRecalc). At least one field within properties must be set. NOTE: To update individual sheet/tab properties (like renaming a specific sheet), use GOOGLESHEETS_UPDATE_SHEET_PROPERTIES instead.

inputs.name: title
inputs.description: The title of the spreadsheet.

inputs.name: locale
inputs.description: The locale of the spreadsheet. Use underscore format (e.g., 'en_US', 'pt_BR'), not hyphenated BCP 47 format (e.g., 'en-US'). Google Sheets API expects underscore-separated locale codes.

inputs.name: timeZone
inputs.description: The time zone of the spreadsheet in CLDR format (e.g., 'America/New_York').

inputs.name: autoRecalc
inputs.description: The recalculation interval for the spreadsheet.

inputs.name: defaultFormat
inputs.description: The default cell format for the entire spreadsheet.

inputs.name: textFormat
inputs.description: Text formatting options.

inputs.name: link

inputs.description: The link URI.

inputs.name: spreadsheetTheme
inputs.description: The theme of the spreadsheet. When updating with field mask 'spreadsheetTheme', provide the complete theme object including both primaryFontFamily and themeColors array with all 9 color types. Per Google Sheets API documentation, all theme color pairs must be provided when updating the theme. Note: Nested field masks (e.g., 'spreadsheetTheme.primaryFontFamily') produce HTTP 400 errors in practice, though not explicitly documented as unsupported.

inputs.name: themeColors
inputs.description: Array of theme color pairs. Each pair contains 'colorType' (TEXT, BACKGROUND, ACCENT1, ACCENT2, ACCENT3, ACCENT4, ACCENT5, ACCENT6, LINK) and 'color' with rgbColor values. All 9 color types must be provided when updating spreadsheetTheme (per Google Sheets API documentation).

inputs.description: The color value.

inputs.description: Controls whether external URL access is permitted for IMPORTRANGE, IMPORTDATA, IMPORTFEED, and IMPORTHTML functions. This field is read-only when true (cannot be disabled once enabled). When false, you can set it to true to enable external URL access. Note: This value may be bypassed if the admin has enabled URL allowlisting at the organization level.

name: Update Table Row Style
description: Tool to update the style of a table row in a Google Document. Use when you need to modify the appearance of specific rows within a table, such as setting minimum row height or marking rows as headers.

inputs.description: The request body for updating the table row style.

inputs.name: fields
inputs.description: The fields that should be updated. At least one field must be specified. The root tableRowStyle is implied and should not be specified. A single "*" can be used as short-hand for listing every field. For example to update the minimum row height, set fields to "minRowHeight".

inputs.name: rowIndices
inputs.description: The list of zero-based row indices whose style should be updated. If no indices are specified, all rows will be updated.

inputs.name: tableRowStyle

inputs.name: minRowHeight
inputs.description: The minimum height of the row. The row will be rendered in the Docs editor at a height equal to or greater than this value in order to show all the content in the row's cells.

inputs.name: unit

inputs.name: preventOverflow

inputs.description: The location where the table starts in the document.

inputs.name: index
inputs.description: The zero-based index, in UTF-16 code units.

inputs.name: tabId
inputs.description: The tab that the location is in. When omitted, the request is applied to the first tab.

inputs.name: segmentId
inputs.description: The ID of the header, footer or footnote the location is in. An empty segment ID signifies the document's body.

name: Update Task (Full Replacement)
description: Tool to fully replace an existing Google Task using PUT method. Use when you need to update the entire task resource, not just specific fields. This method requires all required fields (id, title) and replaces the complete task, unlike PATCH which supports partial updates.

inputs.name: id
inputs.description: Task identifier (REQUIRED). Must match the task_id parameter. This field is required by the Google Tasks API for PUT operations.

inputs.name: due
inputs.description: Due date for the task. NOTE: Google Tasks only stores the date portion (YYYY-MM-DD). Accepts RFC3339 format (e.g., '2025-09-28T00:00:00Z') or date strings like '2025-09-28'.

inputs.name: etag
inputs.description: ETag of the resource for concurrency control. Prevents accidental overwrites when multiple clients modify the same task.

inputs.name: links
inputs.description: Collection of links attached to the task. This collection is read-only.

inputs.name: link
inputs.description: The actual URL or URI. Examples: 'https://docs.example.com/project' or 'mailto:client@example.com'.

inputs.name: type
inputs.description: Type of the link resource. Common values: 'email' for mailto links, 'web' for HTTP/HTTPS URLs, 'keep_note' for Google Keep notes.

inputs.name: description
inputs.description: Human-readable description of what the link points to. May be omitted for certain link types like 'keep_note'. Example: 'Project documentation' or 'Client email'.

inputs.name: notes
inputs.description: Additional details or description for the task. Supports plain text only. Maximum 8192 characters.

inputs.name: title
inputs.description: Title/name of the task (REQUIRED). This is the main text displayed for the task. Maximum 1024 characters.

inputs.name: hidden
inputs.description: Whether the task is hidden. This is read-only and set by the API when task list is cleared. Default: false

inputs.name: parent
inputs.description: Parent task identifier for subtasks. This field is read-only. Use the 'move' method to change parent.

inputs.name: status
inputs.description: Current status of the task. Must be either 'needsAction' (task is pending) or 'completed' (task is done).

inputs.name: deleted
inputs.description: Whether the task has been deleted. Deleted tasks are marked but not immediately removed. Default: false

inputs.name: task_id
inputs.description: Identifier of the Google Task to be fully replaced within the specified task list. This must also be included in the 'id' field of the request body.

inputs.name: position
inputs.description: Position of the task among siblings. This field is read-only. Use the 'move' method to change position.

inputs.name: completed
inputs.description: Date/time when the task was marked as completed. Only applicable when status='completed'. Unlike 'due', this field preserves the time component. Accepts RFC3339 format.

inputs.name: tasklist_id
inputs.description: Identifier of the Google Task list that contains the task to be updated.

requiredInputs.name: id
requiredInputs.description: Task identifier (REQUIRED). Must match the task_id parameter. This field is required by the Google Tasks API for PUT operations.

requiredInputs.name: title
requiredInputs.description: Title/name of the task (REQUIRED). This is the main text displayed for the task. Maximum 1024 characters.

name: Update Team Drive (Deprecated)
description: Tool to update a Team Drive's metadata. Deprecated: Use the drives.update endpoint instead. Use when you need to modify Team Drive properties.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: The name of this Team Drive.

inputs.name: themeId
inputs.description: The ID of the theme from which the background image and color will be set. This is a write-only field; it can only be set on requests that don't set colorRgb or backgroundImageFile.

inputs.name: colorRgb
inputs.description: The color of this Team Drive as an RGB hex string. It can only be set on a drive.teamdrives.update request that does not set themeId.

inputs.name: teamDriveId
inputs.description: The ID of the Team Drive to update.

inputs.name: restrictions
inputs.description: A set of restrictions that apply to this Team Drive or items inside this Team Drive.

inputs.name: domainUsersOnly
inputs.description: Whether access to this Team Drive and items inside this Team Drive is restricted to users of the domain.

inputs.name: teamMembersOnly
inputs.description: Whether access to items inside this Team Drive is restricted to members of this Team Drive.

inputs.name: adminManagedRestrictions
inputs.description: Whether administrative privileges on this Team Drive are required to modify restrictions.

inputs.name: copyRequiresWriterPermission
inputs.description: Whether the options to copy, print, or download files inside this Team Drive should be disabled for readers and commenters.

inputs.name: sharingFoldersRequiresOrganizerPermission
inputs.description: If true, only users with the organizer role can share folders. If false, users with either the organizer role or the file organizer role can share folders.

inputs.name: backgroundImageFile
inputs.description: An image file and cropping parameters from which a background image for this Team Drive is set. This is a write only field; it can only be set on drive.teamdrives.update requests that don't set themeId.

inputs.name: id
inputs.description: The ID of an image file in Drive to use for the background image.

inputs.name: width
inputs.description: The width of the cropped image in the closed range of 0 to 1.

inputs.name: xCoordinate
inputs.description: The X coordinate of the upper left corner of the cropping area in the background image (0 to 1).

inputs.name: yCoordinate
inputs.description: The Y coordinate of the upper left corner of the cropping area in the background image (0 to 1).

inputs.name: useDomainAdminAccess
inputs.description: Issue the request as a domain administrator; if set to true, then the requester will be granted access if they are an administrator of the domain to which the Team Drive belongs.

name: Update Vacation Settings
description: Tool to update vacation responder settings for a Gmail user. Use when you need to configure out-of-office auto-replies.

inputs.name: userId
inputs.description: The user's email address. The special value 'me' can be used to indicate the authenticated user.

inputs.name: endTime
inputs.description: An optional end time for sending auto-replies (epoch ms). When this is specified, Gmail will automatically reply only to messages that it receives before the end time. If both startTime and endTime are specified, startTime must precede endTime.

inputs.name: startTime
inputs.description: An optional start time for sending auto-replies (epoch ms). When this is specified, Gmail will automatically reply only to messages that it receives after the start time. If both startTime and endTime are specified, startTime must precede endTime.

inputs.name: enableAutoReply
inputs.description: Flag that controls whether Gmail automatically replies to messages.

inputs.name: responseSubject
inputs.description: Optional text to prepend to the subject line in vacation responses. In order to enable auto-replies, either the response subject or the response body must be nonempty.

inputs.name: responseBodyHtml
inputs.description: Response body in HTML format. Gmail will sanitize the HTML before storing it. If both response_body_plain_text and response_body_html are specified, response_body_html will be used.

inputs.name: restrictToDomain
inputs.description: Flag that determines whether responses are sent to recipients who are outside of the user's domain. This feature is only available for Google Workspace users.

inputs.name: restrictToContacts
inputs.description: Flag that determines whether responses are sent to recipients who are not in the user's list of contacts.

inputs.name: responseBodyPlainText
inputs.description: Response body in plain text format. If both response_body_plain_text and response_body_html are specified, response_body_html will be used.
